runners.name: Sun Goddess
runners.spotlight: 120,000gns Sioux Nation filly; went very close on Naas debut (5f, good) last month and took a big step forward with her emphatic win in a Curragh maiden (6f, good) 15 days later; sets the standard on that form and she's a strong contender for yard that won this 2016, 2022 and 2024.
runners.comment: Sets the standard on her Curragh win; yard won this in 2016, 2022 and 2024; strong claims

runners.name: Valentina Bella
runners.spotlight: Won at Bordeaux Le Bouscat (5f, good to soft) on debut and followed up in a Listed race at Vichy (5f, soft) last month; this is a different test upped in trip but she has some useful form and a French raider won this race in 2017.
runners.comment: Listed winner in France last month and a French raider won this race in 2017

runners.name: Dark Issue
runners.spotlight: 11-1 for a Goodwood maiden (6f, good) last month but she made steady headway before storming clear for a 5l win; that form is not easy to weigh up but the style was impressive and she should improve for the experience.
runners.comment: 11-1 at Goodwood (6f, good) last month but she stormed clear for a 5l win; not ruled out

runners.name: What A Girl Wants
runners.spotlight: Three promising efforts in Ireland include a close call behind a stablemate at Naas (5f, good) last time; this is much tougher on her British/stable debut but she has a progressive profile and is still with Amo Racing; not ruled out upped in trip.
runners.comment: 0-3 in Ireland but she has a progressive profile; not ruled out upped in trip for new yard

runners.name: Jolivette
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett half-sister to 7f/1m winner Jonquil (including 2yo/Group 2); 2-1 favourite for a fillies' novice at Newmarket (6f, good) last month and travelled smoothly before knuckling down well to beat Topaz with a bit in hand; that was a bright start and she's in the mix for a yard that won this in 2021.
runners.comment: Made a bright start when winning at Newmarket; in the mix for yard that won this in 2021

runners.name: Love Is
runners.spotlight: 90,000euros half-sister to four winners; scored in good style on Windsor debut (6f, good to firm) last month and her second of three at Goodwood (6f, good to soft) last time was behind a smart prospect; should have more to offer but she needs improvement in this Group 3 event.
runners.comment: Windsor winner who ran into a smart prospect at Goodwood latest; needs to find more

runners.name: Libertango
runners.spotlight: 400,000gns breeze-up 2yo; 4-1, took a bit of time to figure things out at Leicester (6f, good to firm) last month but finished well for a comfortable success over a clear second; that was a performance packed with potential and she has to be feared.
runners.comment: 400,000gns breeze-up 2yo; 4-1 when a comfortable winner at Leicester from a clear second

runners.name: Topaz
runners.spotlight: Persian Force filly who attracted support into 13-2 at Newmarket (6f, good) last month and came from rear to give Jolivette a scare in the closing stages; that was a promising debut and she looks a nice prospect.
runners.comment: Close second behind Jolivette at Newmarket and she looks a nice prospect

runners.name: Light Of Dawn
runners.spotlight: 625,000euros breeze-up purchase; showed signs of greenness in a four-runner novice at Carlisle (6f, soft) 11 days ago but she landed the odds in smooth style; this is a different test but she's bred to be smart and her yard won this with a runner with a similar profile last year.
runners.comment: Smooth winner at Carlisle and yard won this with a runner with a similar profile last year

runners.name: Acclamation Star
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns yearling; 17-2 at Bath (5.7f, firm) three weeks ago but she got the hang of things in the closing stages and flew home to score by just over 3l; that was a striking debut but she has plenty to find on this big step up in class.
runners.comment: Clearcut win at Bath three weeks ago but she has lots to find on this big step up in class

runners.name: Silent Beauty
runners.spotlight: 1,000,000euros yearling; dam 7f-1m winner (including 2yo/Group 1; RPR 111); 5-4 favourite at Yarmouth (6f, good to soft) last month and travelled strongly before surging clear for an easy win; that was an impressive debut and she could have a lot more to offer; big player.
runners.comment: 1,000,000euros yearling; impressive winner at Yarmouth and she's an interesting contender

runners.name: Kochella
runners.spotlight: 72,000gns breeze-up 2yo; sole filly in the line-up at Pontefract (6f, good) 17 days ago and she set the pace before finishing a close third of seven; that was a solid start but she looks a minor player upped to this grade.
runners.comment: Showed promise at Pontefract but she looks a minor player upped to this grade

runners.name: Ron's Angel
runners.spotlight: Went close on Bath debut (5f, good) last month before landing the odds in a fillies' maiden at Lingfield (6f, AW) two weeks ago; battled well last time but she didn't need to improve to take that prize and she's well down the pecking order on form in this line-up.
runners.comment: Won a fillies' maiden at Lingfield but she's well down the pecking order on form here

runners.name: Crazy Cubana
runners.spotlight: 120,000gns half-sister to winners Lancillotto (French 1m4f) and Bibiche (French 2m1f hurdle); looked in trouble at the halfway stage at Nottingham (6f, good) last month but she finished well to snatch an unlikely victory at 12-1; showed a good attitude in that race but the form hasn't worked out and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Snatched unlikely victory at Nottingham (6f, good; 12-1) but that form hasn't worked out

runners.name: Dubai Charm
runners.spotlight: Promising second on Nottingham debut (6f, good) in April but she failed to build on that when a 6l third at Yarmouth (6f, good to soft) last month; passed over.
runners.comment: Promising second on Nottingham debut but she failed to build on that at Yarmouth

runners.name: Dee's Funny Girl
runners.spotlight: Overcame trouble when scoring at 16-1 at Thirsk (5f, good) last month but the runner-up was beaten in a claimer next time; this looks a daunting task on the figures but she was sold for £150,000 at Goffs London sale on Monday.
runners.comment: Overcame trouble for 16-1 win at Thirsk; sold for £150,000 at Goffs London sale on Monday

runners.name: Magnesium
runners.spotlight: Improved on her 5f debut effort when winning a Kempton novice (6f, AW) last month but that form is modest and she looks outclassed against the potential in this field.
runners.comment: Found improvement when scoring at Kempton but that form is modest; passed over

runners.name: Bated Benevolence
runners.spotlight: Bated Breath filly who made a good start when justifying support in Nottingham maiden (6f, good; 13-2) last month; should benefit from that experience but this is a tough assignment and she needs major improvement.
runners.comment: Won on Nottingham debut last month but this is tough and she needs major improvement

runners.name: King's Prize
runners.spotlight: Too Darn Hot filly out of a Listed-placed 1m2f winner (RPR 106); well backed into 4-1 for Nottingham debut (6f, good) in April and she stayed on strongly to win in good style; not sure how strong that form is but it was a professional display and she looks an exciting prospect.
runners.comment: Won in good style on Nottingham debut and she looks an exciting prospect
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Hidden Gift
runners.spotlight: £150,000 breeze-up 2yo; second favourite on Ripon debut (5f, good) two weeks ago but started slowly before coming from last to finish a close second of seven behind a rival who was always well positioned; looks open to plenty of progress upped in trip and she's out of a sister to a 6f Group 3 juvenile winner.
runners.comment: Major eye-catcher at Ripon and she's open to plenty of progress upped in trip

runners.name: Glamorize
runners.spotlight: £155,000 breeze-up 2yo; attracted support into 15-2 and showed promise when runner-up behind an impressive rival, who ran well in the Queen Mary on Wednesday, at Lingfield (5f, good to firm) last month; should be more streetwise this time but she needs lots of improvement upped in trip.
runners.comment: £155,000 breeze-up 2yo; runner-up at Lingfield but she needs lots of improvement here

runners.name: Tall Trees
runners.spotlight: Ran green and flashed her tail early on but she made some good late headway when fifth at Newbury (6.5f, good to firm) 18 days ago; that was a promising effort at a big price but whether she's up to this severe rise in class is another matter.
runners.comment: Showed promise when fifth at Newbury but she looks opposable on this severe rise in class

runners.name: Blue Sign
runners.spotlight: 16-1 and always behind after a slow start when a well-held fourth at Lingfield (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago.
runners.comment: 16-1 and always behind when a well-held fourth on Lingfield debut

runners.name: Roxelina
runners.spotlight: 170,000gns foal by Havana Grey; showed promise when third at Windsor (6f, good to soft) 11 days ago but she couldn't justify favouritism in that race and and this is a much more demanding test.
runners.comment: Held in third when favourite at Windsor and this is a much sterner examination

runners.name: Desert Sands
runners.spotlight: 16-1 for recent Newbury debut (6.5f, good to firm) and she faded into a well-held sixth of 12.
runners.comment: 16-1 for recent Newbury debut (6.5f, good to firm) and she faded into a well-held sixth

runners.name: Wise Approach
runners.spotlight: Runner-up to Charles Darwin in the Norfolk here (5f, good to firm) 12 months ago and won the Group 1 Middle Park at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) in September; hasn't hit the same heights in races won by Coppull and Song Of The Clyde this season but he's not written off.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner last September; hasn't hit the same heights this season but not written off

runners.name: Venetian Sun
runners.spotlight: 5-5 over sprint trips, including 2yo wins at this meeting (C&D, good to firm) and in the Group 1 Prix Morny at Deauville (6f, good); having failed to stay 1m in the 1,000 Guineas, she was dropped back in trip in the Group 2 Sandy Lane Stakes at Haydock (6f, good to soft) four weeks ago and won in fine style; leading claims.
runners.comment: 2yo winner at this meeting and in Group 1; won Sandy Lane in fine style; leading claims

runners.name: Havana Anna
runners.spotlight: Runner-up to subsequent 1,000 Guineas winner True Love in the Group 1 Cheveley Park at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last September and travelled well when a gutsy winner of the Group 3 Lacken Stakes at Naas (6f, good) on her reappearance; could build on that latest run and give a good account.
runners.comment: Group 1 runner-up last September and Group 3 winner on comeback; could give good account

runners.name: Division
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at York (6f, good) last October and reappeared with close third behind Coppull and Midnight Tango in the Group 3 Trial over this C&D (good to firm), finishing strongly after blowing the start; failed to settle and perhaps not seen to best effect when 3l second to Venetian Sun in Group 2 Sandy Lane Stakes at Haydock (6f, good to soft) last time; could go well.
runners.comment: Placed in Group races on both starts this season (including C&D) and could go well

runners.name: Zanthos
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed the run of the race against the rail when making virtually all to win the Group 2 Rockfel at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) last September on her third start, but there was still lots to like about that performance and the runner-up went on to finish fourth in the 1,000 Guineas; soundly beaten in the French 1,000 Guineas (1m, heavy) on sole outing this year but she's lightly raced and could be suited by this first crack at sprinting; chance.
runners.comment: Group 2 Rockfel winner who could be suited by this first crack at sprinting; chance

runners.name: Albert Einstein
runners.spotlight: 2-2 in truncated 2yo campaign, including 6f Group 3; 0-3 this year but appeared suited by the drop back to 6f when neck second to Song Of The Clyde in Listed race at Newbury (good) last month, faring best of those who raced in the centre group; improvement needed but he's Ryan Moore's mount of the Ballydoyle three and could still have more to offer; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Listed 2nd last time; needs improvement but could still have more to offer for top yard

runners.name: Charles Darwin
runners.spotlight: Impressive in the Group 2 Norfolk Stakes here (5f, good to firm) last June on fourth and final 2yo start; reappeared with Listed win at Navan (6f, good to yielding) in April and he was eased at Naas last month after Ryan Moore felt he lost his action; Moore rides stablemate Albert Einstein but this fellow remains one to be interested in; wears first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Impressive in the Norfolk here last June; mixed fortunes since but remains of interest

runners.name: Samangan
runners.spotlight: Won Group 3 and Group 2 races over 6f at Chantilly (good to soft/soft) last autumn and reappeared with solid second in Group 3 at Deauville (7f, good to soft); perhaps unsuited by heavy ground when below par at Chantilly last month and he retains potential, but others have stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Two Group-race wins last autumn; retains potential but others have stronger credentials

runners.name: Kimi Rey
runners.spotlight: Two-time French Listed winner who has finished close up in Group 3s at Chantilly on her last two runs (5.5f/6f, good/heavy), a head behind My Calyx Cen on the first occasion; however, she has to produce something new today.
runners.comment: Close up in Group 3s at Chantilly on last two starts but needs to produce something new

runners.name: Brussels
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two top-level races last autumn, namely the Middle Park at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) and Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint (5f, firm); a long way below that level on both outings this term and arrives with plenty to prove but he's an each-way possible if cheekpieces prompt a revival.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two top-level races last autumn; cheekpieces need to prompt a revival

runners.name: Fitzella
runners.spotlight: Made all in the Group 3 Princess Margaret Stakes over C&D (good) last July; some good runs in defeat since but she again looks vulnerable today.
runners.comment: Group 3 C&D winner last July; some good runs in defeat since but again looks vulnerable

runners.name: My Calyx Cen
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m/6.5f earlier this season and made it four wins from his five starts when landing a Group 3 by a neck at Chantilly in April (5.5f, good; from Kimi Rey); he needs another step forward today but this French challenger is progressive and might have more left in the tank; not discounted.
runners.comment: Progressive French challenger who won a Group 3 last time and is not discounted

runners.name: Outfielder
runners.spotlight: No match for the front three (including Venetian Sun and Wise Approach) when fourth of six in the Group 1 Prix Morny at Deauville (6f, good) last August but unbeaten over sprint trips otherwise; he's posted ready wins in two conditions races in the US this year and his US trainer has enjoyed plenty of success at Royal Ascot, winning this race in 2021; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Ready wins in two conditions races in the USA this year and he's not ruled out

runners.name: Song Of The Clyde
runners.spotlight: Won a sales race at York (6f, good to firm) last August and his form went to a new level when an all-the-way Listed winner at Newbury (6f, good) last month, narrowly from Albert Einstein; this is his first Group-race assignment and further improvement is needed but he's an each-way possible.
runners.comment: Form went to new level with Listed win at Newbury and he's an each-way possible
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Cxx & M Parker

runners.name: Spicy Marg
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at Newmarket (6f, good) last October; respectable efforts this season when midfield in the 1,000 Guineas (1m, good to firm) and third in the Sandy Lane at Haydock (6f, good to soft) but she was no match for the winner Venetian Sun last time and today's task may well be beyond her.
runners.comment: 2yo Listed winner; respectable efforts this season but no match for Venetian Sun last time

runners.name: Coppull
runners.spotlight: Third in the Coventry here (6f, good to firm) last June before 2l win in the Group 2 Richmond at Goodwood (6f, good), and campaign concluded with close third in the Group 1 Middle Park at Newmarket (6f, good to firm); reappeared with Group 3 win in the Trial over C&D (good to firm) and he's respected.
runners.comment: Won the Group 3 Trial over C&D on his reappearance and he's respected

runners.name: Rock On Thunder
runners.spotlight: Second in the Gimcrack at York (6f, good to firm) last August but well beaten in the Mill Reef at Newbury (6f, good to soft) next time and same story in the Sandy Lane at Haydock (6f, good to soft) last month; cheekpieces and the return to fast ground could help but others have less to prove.
runners.comment: Second in the Gimcrack at York last August but well beaten on both starts since
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Havana Hurricane
runners.spotlight: 0-5 since winning the Listed Windsor Castle Stakes here 12 months ago (5f, good to firm) and couldn't threaten Song Of The Clyde and Albert Einstein when third in Listed race at Newbury (6f, good) on reappearance; however, he was held up in a race in which the first two were in front throughout, and today's big field and stiff finish may well see him to better effect.
runners.comment: Listed 3rd behind two of these at Newbury; could be seen to better effect in this scenario

runners.name: Northern Champion
runners.spotlight: Won two conditions races at Meydan (7f/6f, good) early this year then finished down the field against older horses in the Group 1 Al Quoz Sprint there (6f, good) in March; Jamie Spencer being on board is a positive on the straight course here but this 3yo has something to find.
runners.comment: Two conditions race wins at Meydan this year but he has something to find in this line-up

runners.name: Aspect Island
runners.spotlight: Third in Group 3 Cornwallis (5f, good) and Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint (5f, firm) on final two 2yo starts, and also third in Listed race at York (5f, good) last month; respectable midfield finish in King Charles III Stakes here (5f, good to firm) on Tuesday but needs a career best today.
runners.comment: Midfield in King Charles III Stakes here on Tuesday; needs a career best today

runners.name: Midnight Tango
runners.spotlight: 0-6 in Group/Listed races since winning a maiden at Hamilton last June on her second start but she's often run well and was better than ever when a neck runner-up to Coppull in the Group 3 Trial over C&D (good to firm) on last month's return; each-way claims if backing up that performance.
runners.comment: Neck second to Coppull in the C&D Trial and each-way claims if backing up that performance

runners.name: Super Soldier
runners.spotlight: Eight runs last season, the highlight his 6f Group 2 near-miss at Chantilly in July; however, he was inconsistent and hasn't shown anything like enough on his two runs this term to suggest he'll make an impact today.
runners.comment: Group 2 near-miss at Chantilly last July but very opposable on this season's evidence

runners.name: Hopewell Rock
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who's without a handicap win but has mostly progressed in defeat, with the exception on heavy going; came from last to third in eight-runner race at Newmarket (1m4f, good to firm) seven weeks ago for a promising reappearance and the two ahead of him filled the same positions over 1m6f here on Tuesday; changed hands since and has a major chance.
runners.comment: Promising reappearance third at Newmarket (1m4f, good to firm) behind Tuesday's one-two

runners.name: Opportunity
runners.spotlight: Off 11 months after tailing off in a Group 2 at this meeting last year; shaped promisingly on handicap debut over C&D on return and firmly in charge through the final 2f at Carlisle (1m3f, again good to firm) three weeks later; trying to avoid trouble in running from stall 1 looks a headache today but zero shock if the 8lb rise underestimates him.
runners.comment: Firmly in charge through the final 2f at Carlisle (1m3f, good to firm); up 8lb and stall 1

runners.name: Warrant Holder
runners.spotlight: Won in good style in a small field at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) last October in his second handicap; gelded and tongue tied when returning over 1m2f in 15-runner handicap at York (good; unraced on firmer) five weeks ago and he won that too, racing freely in mid-division before going on over 1f out to score by 1l; up 5lb and needs to settle better but clear potential still.
runners.comment: 1m4f/1m2f wins on last two starts; needs to settle better but has clear potential still
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Plage De Havre
runners.spotlight: 10lb higher than when winning a valuable 1m4f handicap at Haydock (good; probably acts on good to firm) last July in great style; that was his final race last season but he put up a bold show when third at York five weeks ago and should be primed for another.
runners.comment: Put up a bold show when third at York five weeks ago and should be primed for another

runners.name: Military Academy
runners.spotlight: 1m4f AW Listed winner for the Gosdens but he left that yard under a cloud in the autumn; rediscovered some form at 1m2f on AW last winter but reverted to lesser efforts on his four outings this spring.
runners.comment: Rediscovered some form at 1m2f on AW last winter but has reverted to lesser efforts

runners.name: Insanity
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who mostly ran well last season and did so again when second at Pontefract (1m2f) on reappearance this April; each-way chance if he can avoid trouble from an inside stall.
runners.comment: C&D winner; mostly runs well; each-way chance if he can avoid trouble from an inside stall

runners.name: Regal Ulixes
runners.spotlight: Well-beaten seventh in 1m4f Group 2 at this meeting on final run last year; won handicap at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in February, has held his form and had possible excuses, while also appearing as if he's well worth another go at 1m4f, so he's given a second look despite needing overall improvement.
runners.comment: Worth another go at 1m4f, so he's given a second look despite needing overall improvement

runners.name: Teumessias Fox
runners.spotlight: 6th, 15th and 11th in his three 1m4f handicaps at this meeting; 11-week break is not a concern but slow starts have become an unwelcome habit and he's proved far more potent on AW than turf in recent years, so probably won't be able just yet to exploit what should be a favourable mark.
runners.comment: It should be a favourable mark, but far more potent on AW than turf in recent years

runners.name: Serengeti
runners.spotlight: In his third yard since leaving Aidan O'Brien last autumn; third run for current trainer when upped to 1m2f and tongue tied first time at Windsor (good to firm) last month, starting 50-1 in a six-runner race but leading 1f out and narrowly holding off two challengers; back up just 1lb but this is another new trip and it's a vastly more competitive race.
runners.comment: 50-1, upped to 1m2f and tongue tied first time when narrow winner at Windsor last time

runners.name: Emit
runners.spotlight: His 1m2f Group 3 run here (40-1) was the only blip from him last season; close second in 1m7f French Group 2 last October looks his best form but his handicap mark has fallen and plenty of ability remains; the biggest question may be whether this trip is now on the short side.
runners.comment: Plenty of ability remains; biggest question may be whether this trip is now on short side

runners.name: Nightime Dancer
runners.spotlight: Ran creditably last summer in the Derby and two 1m5f Group 3s, while coming up well short; absent 259 days before his fourth of eight on handicap debut at Newmarket (1m4f, good to firm; Hopewell Rock third) seven weeks ago, which was actually his best form and the first two also dominated a 1m6f handicap here on Tuesday, but he needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Fourth of eight on handicap debut at Newmarket (1m4f; Hopewell Rock third) seven weeks ago

runners.name: Omni Man
runners.spotlight: Creditable fifth in the 3yo handicap over 1m4f at this meeting last year, which he followed with a comfortable 1m4f win at Glorious Goodwood (8lb higher today); needs to resume his progress, having been a never-dangerous outsider this spring in 1m2f Group 3 and 1m Listed contest, but he looks far more interesting for this race.
runners.comment: Needs to resume progress but looks far more interesting than in his two races this spring

runners.name: Nesthorn
runners.spotlight: 2-5 in France for Francis Graffard; also scored in December in Bahrain for new connections but managed only seventh in his three subsequent runs there and, after 11 weeks off, has more to prove racing beyond 1m2f for first time.
runners.comment: Only 7th in last three runs in Bahrain; 11 weeks off and races beyond 1m2f for first time

runners.name: Dain Ma Nut In
runners.spotlight: Exuberant and game front-runner who scored twice over 1m2f at York (good/good to firm) last summer, including at their big meeting, and he also won one of his three races in Bahrain in the winter; absent four months and whether he can stretch his stamina to 1m4f (he didn't at 1m3f on latest start) looks a major doubt.
runners.comment: Exuberant and game front-runner over 1m2f; off four months and new trip may stretch him

runners.name: Burdett Road
runners.spotlight: Won 1m2f handicap at this meeting in 2023 and has also tasted the high life in top hurdle races; attentions were turned back to the Flat for four races (UAE and Saudi Arabia) this January-March and he wasn't in peak form, but edged down the weights as a result and this drop back in trip can help.
runners.comment: 2023 winner here; has edged down the weights and this drop back in trip can help

runners.name: French Duke
runners.spotlight: Sixth and eighth off lower marks in 1m4f handicaps at the last two Royal Ascots (good to firm) for Roger Varian; won over 1m4f at Meydan in January on debut for new trainer, followed by a lesser show at Abu Dhabi in February; usually a free-going sort, so he's likely to bide his time in rear before trying for a late challenge; most form suggests he won't succeed with it.
runners.comment: Royal Ascot 6th and 8th off lower marks for R Varian; won first of two runs for new yard

runners.name: Ambiente Friendly
runners.spotlight: His second in the 2024 Derby is feeling pretty distant now and on latest outing he was second of four in a handicap hurdle at Fakenham; hooded throughout that hurdles campaign and sometimes on Flat, but that is now replaced by cheekpieces; this is also his first ever Flat handicap.
runners.comment: His star has fallen dramatically; cheekpieces on and this is his first ever Flat handicap

runners.name: Tuscan Hills
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced for three trainers and little seems to have gone right since he went 2-2 as a 2yo; the positives seem to be that he was considered worth a shot at the Dante and Derby last season, moves back up from 1m and away from heavy ground.
runners.comment: Lightly raced for three trainers and little seems to have gone right since 2-2 as a 2yo

runners.name: Mondo Man
runners.spotlight: Free-going sort but the efforts to make a success of him over hurdles finally paid off this year with his front-running win in the Imperial Cup at Sandown in March; smart in Group races on the Flat in 2024 and May 2025, one to consider seriously in this first Flat handicap.
runners.comment: Formerly smart in Group races and is one to consider seriously in this first Flat handicap

runners.name: Precise
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive 2yo last season, which concluded with two Group 1 wins (7f/1m, yielding/good), and she bounced back from an underwhelming reappearance in the 1,000 Guineas at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) to win the Irish version at the Curragh (1m, good) by just over 2l from stablemate and 1,000 Guineas winner True Love; won a Group 3 on fast ground; she's the one to beat.
runners.comment: Beat 1,000 Guineas winner True Love to win the Irish 1,000 Guineas; she's the one to beat

runners.name: True Love
runners.spotlight: Kept busy in 2yo campaign, which featured wins in the Queen Mary at this meeting (5f, good to firm) and the Group 1 Cheveley Park at Newmarket (6f, good to firm); posted second Group 1 win in the 1,000 Guineas at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) then came second to Precise in the Irish version at the Curragh (1m, good), beaten about 2l; work to do to turn the tables on her stablemate but she maybe wasn't at her very best last time; the standout danger.
runners.comment: Won 1,000 Guineas and second to Precise in the Irish version; the standout danger
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Balantina
runners.spotlight: This is her first run of the year but she won the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies Turf at Del Mar (1m, firm) last October when last seen; ran well in third in the Albany here 12 months ago; can give a good account if fully tuned up.
runners.comment: This is first run of the year but she won at the Breeders' Cup last October when last seen
runners.owner: S I Weston/Medallion Racing LLC/Reeves T'bred/ L A McNulty

runners.name: Sukanya
runners.spotlight: Improved upon her 2yo form when coming from the back of the pack to win the Group 3 Fred Darling Stakes at Newbury (7f, good) on her reappearance; well beaten at 25-1 in the Irish 1,000 Guineas at the Curragh (1m, good) but she was bumped at the start and failed to settle; she may have untapped potential at 1m if settling better and she's not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: Won Group 3 before well beaten in Irish Guineas, but had an excuse; not ruled out each-way

runners.name: Black Caviar Gold
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner at the Curragh (7f, soft) last September and runner-up in the same grade there (1m, good) early last month; however, she was behind Precise and True Love when fourth in the Irish 1,000 Guineas at the Curragh (1m, good) last time, beaten about 6l, and improvement is needed; a first-time visor replaces the cheekpieces worn on her last two starts.
runners.comment: 2yo Group 3 winner; held in fourth in Irish 1,000 Guineas and improvement is needed

runners.name: Touleen
runners.spotlight: 2-3 in 2yo campaign and close second to Sukanya in the Group 3 Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good) on her return; another good run when sixth of 19 to True Love in the 1,000 Guineas at Newmarket (1m, good to firm), beaten just over 4l; further improvement is necessary if she's to pose a serious threat but she's in excellent hands to continue to progress; might not be far away.
runners.comment: Sixth of 19 in the 1,000 Guineas; improvement needed but she may well progress further

runners.name: Timeforshowcasing
runners.spotlight: Best form has come on AW/slow turf and well beaten on good on firm in the 1,000 Guineas at Newmarket, while she also has something to find with the principals on her peak runs; however, she won the German 1,000 Guineas (1m, soft) 19 days ago and an uncontested lead is a distinct possibility today, which would be a major plus; could make a bold bid if handling conditions.
runners.comment: Won the German 1,000 Guineas; uncontested lead a distinct possibility; could make bold bid

runners.name: Rose Ghaiyyath
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Deauville (7f, soft) last August and promising fourth in Group 2 May Hill at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) in September; however, only tenth in the 1,000 Guineas (1m, good to firm) on reappearance and soundly beaten in Listed race at Goodwood (1m2f, soft) since; others preferred.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on both starts this year (including 1,000 Guineas); others preferred

runners.name: Moon Target
runners.spotlight: Runner-up to Precise in the Group 3 Prestige Stakes at Goodwood (7f, good to firm) last August and third in the Group 2 Rockfel at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) in September; she's better than she showed in the Group 1 Fillies' Mile at Newmarket (1m, good) last October, when well beaten behind Precise, but this is her first run since and she looks up against it.
runners.comment: Placed in Group races last season but looks up against it on her reappearance

runners.name: Glyfada
runners.spotlight: Irish Oaks entrant who won a maiden at Naas (10.3f, good) in April on her second start and coped fine with the drop to 1m when following up at Killarney (good; won going away) at the expense of a subsequent winner; this is a huge hike in class but she's promising and the booking of Oisin Murphy is a statement of intent.
runners.comment: Won her last two in Ireland and booking of Oisin Murphy is a statement of intent

runners.name: Darn Hot Gallop
runners.spotlight: Maintained her unbeaten record when beating male opposition in a 1m handicap at Newmarket that's worked out well (1m, good); it was then always going to be tough for her in the 1,000 Guineas for which she went off 80-1; only 3lb above her winning mark, she's one to bear in mind.
runners.comment: Outclassed last time in the Guineas; only 3lb higher than for previous handicap win

runners.name: Rosa Inglesa
runners.spotlight: Yet another runner who produced career-best form in winning last time out, in her case a Class 4 at Nottingham (extended 1m; good to soft; fine on faster); a 9lb rise only goes to show how emphatic a performance that was; trainer won this in 2019 and 2020.
runners.comment: Yet another runner who produced career-best form in winning last time out; raised 9lb

runners.name: Dream Camp
runners.spotlight: This 250,000euros yearling has come via the quiet roads to Ascot but she might be peaking at the right time having won as she liked at Southwell last month (1m maiden, AW); can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Might be peaking at the right time having won as she liked at Southwell last month

runners.name: Lyrics Of Life
runners.spotlight: Not your typical six-race maiden as she had notable form last season, including a second behind Oaks runner-up Legacy Link and a close fifth in a Group 3; this season's defeats have come in races won by Gosden filly So Regal who is going places; proven on fast ground and no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Not your typical six-race maiden as she has notable form, last season and this term

runners.name: Seet
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five winners including the high-class middle-distance performer Kalpana; made her debut in only April and has since comfortably won minor races at Wolverhampton (8.6f) and Doncaster (1m, good to firm); while there's always a fear that this huge field on handicap debut will be a culture shock, her pedigree and progress suggest she could be heading for Group races.
runners.comment: Huge field on handicap debut might be a culture shock but there's no denying her potential

runners.name: True Test
runners.spotlight: Has gone 0-6 since winning her first two races last summer; competitive at Meydan earlier in the year but she was 250-1 for the 1,000 Guineas at Newmarket and never counted; others are probably on better terms with the handicapper.
runners.comment: Never counted in the 1,000 Guineas and is looking exposed; others might be better in

runners.name: Bintaziza
runners.spotlight: Unchallenged in her first two races on the AW at about 1m and nothing went right from a bad draw on handicap debut at Thirsk (1m, good; 9-2); others have achieved heaps more but that's reflected in her mark and she's in the right hands to do better.
runners.comment: Won her first two and nothing went right on handicap debut; she's a dark one

runners.name: Mubasimah
runners.spotlight: Went close in a Group 3 (7f, good) last season on only her second career start; no fireworks as yet this campaign when only sixth in the Nell Gwyn, nowhere in the 1,000 Guineas and a fading fifth from the front in a French Group 2 last time (1m, good); with limitations becoming exposed she's dropped into a handicap, but others are surely more progressive.
runners.comment: Good 2yo form but doesn't look as progressive as some of the runners in this field

runners.name: Thankfully
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of a Beverley maiden before finishing fifth on her handicap debut at Thirsk (1m, good); she may struggle to reverse those placings with Bintaziza who had excuses.
runners.comment: Narrow winner of a Beverley maiden before finishing fifth on her handicap debut at Thirsk
runners.owner: Mrs Tabor, Mrs Mv Magnier, Mrs Shanahan

runners.name: Zooming
runners.spotlight: Won her novice last season at Newmarket (7f, good to soft); never that dangerous but was running on when fifth in the Group 3 Fred Darling (7f, good) and it was back over 6f when unable to land any sort of blow in a Listed race last month; lightly raced filly in a top stable and pedigree suggests that this first crack at 1m should be beneficial; makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: Low mileage and pedigree suggests that this first crack at 1m should be beneficial

runners.name: Green Carrera
runners.spotlight: One of two runners for Joseph O'Brien; has come from well behind at the Curragh (7f/1m, good) in both her handicaps, scraping home by the skin of her teeth in the latest; this weight would be borderline for Dylan Browne McMonagle who has ridden this filly on her last two starts but now partners her stablemate.
runners.comment: One of two runners for Joseph O'Brien; scraped home at the Curragh last time

runners.name: Spinning Around
runners.spotlight: Has had seven races but won two of her last three, 7f handicaps at Dundalk (AW; cheekpieces) and the Curragh (good; tongue tied); went up 5lb for the last of those (workmanlike in beating Green Carrera) and they claimed 5lb that day, so she has plenty more on her plate here.
runners.comment: Won two of her last three but these weights are tougher than at the Curragh last time
runners.owner: Mrs M V Magnier,Mrs O'Loughlin,Mrs Byrne

runners.name: Just Call Me Angel
runners.spotlight: Has had eight races and posted career win number four in Italy's 1,000 Guineas (good; narrowly); back down to earth in a Listed race at Goodwood where perhaps 1m2f proved a stretch; returns to what is probably her best trip but with a fairly exposed profile.
runners.comment: Italian 1,000 Guineas winner; back at her best trip but with a fairly exposed profile

runners.name: Blingy's Sister
runners.spotlight: 0-4 since debut win and hasn't threatened to take a hand in Group 3 races on last two outings; absent for two months and doesn't look on that good a mark; Toby Moore's claim will at least help in that respect.
runners.comment: 0-4 since debut win; hasn't threatened to take a hand in Group 3 races on last two runs

runners.name: Symbol Of Majesty
runners.spotlight: Narrowly prevailed in two 1m novices (AW/good to firm) and got messed about in a muddling race when a beaten favourite (at odds of 6-5) on handicap debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f); has kept a low profile thus far but interesting that she once held a Group 1 Coronation Stakes entry; intriguing runner.
runners.comment: Won two 1m novices before an excusable defeat on her handicap debut

runners.name: Quiet Mutiny
runners.spotlight: Took the notable scalp of Classic heroine Precise in a Fairyhouse 6f maiden last season (good); found 5.5f too sharp on  return and was then a closing fourth on handicap debut at the Curragh where the 7f proved well within reach; so too might this 1m; respected.
runners.comment: Beat Precise in a maiden; closing fourth upped to 1m on last month's handicap debut

runners.name: Repel
runners.spotlight: French challenger who isn't the easiest to assess, having won twice (good/soft) at odds-on either side of finishing well held in a Group 3 at Deauville; the fact she's lightly raced and in top hands means she can't be discounted on handicap debut, and her half-sister Crenelle was third in this four years ago; stall 1 might not be the place to be.
runners.comment: 2-4 in France, one of the defeats in a Group 3; lightly raced and well bred

runners.name: She Commands
runners.spotlight: Another unexposed filly from a top yard and she cost 450,000gns; her only turf run was on soft ground on debut and she's looked a work in progress in winning two on the AW (1m/9.4f), last time at the expense of a subsequent winner; the potential is there to take a sizeable step forward in her first handicap.
runners.comment: Two novice wins; potential is there to take a sizeable step forward in her first handicap

runners.name: Harlequin Angel
runners.spotlight: Outclassed in a sales race in October but progressive otherwise and had plenty in hand when winning a Class 4 7f handicap under Alexandra Egan at Yarmouth in April (good to firm; 6lb lower); should win further races but opposable in this one.
runners.comment: Class 4 winner last time; can add further races but very opposable in this one

runners.name: Brigid's Well
runners.spotlight: Made all in a 1m novice as a 2yo and very nearly did so in another; comfortably held in Listed races on her last two appearances, latterly when tongue tied and essentially disappointing at Newmarket (1m2f; first run on ground quicker than good); it asks a lot of her to bounce back in a race of this nature.
runners.comment: Comfortably held in Listed races on her last two appearances; enough to prove

runners.name: Crownright
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly; best run was the second, on the round course here over 1m (good to firm) when splitting a subsequent Listed-placed filly and Lyrics Of Life; that was her only run in a hood, which was missing (as is the case here) when a disappointing favourite behind Harlequin Breeze at Leicester; if able to replicate that penultimate effort, she wouldn't be a lost cause off 8st 5lb.
runners.comment: If she could replicate her penultimate effort, she wouldn't be a lost cause off 8st 5lb.

runners.name: Mixed Feelings
runners.spotlight: Won her maiden at Leopardstown by 6l (1m, yielding to soft; runner-up beaten since and now 0-4); it's unwise to suggest that her limitations were exposed in an Oaks trial over further at Naas as that race never went entirely to plan; her very low draw could be troublesome but she's one of a bucketload in here capable of better.
runners.comment: Impressed in her maiden; fifth in an Oaks trial but that race never went to plan

runners.name: Hope Queen
runners.spotlight: Odds-on debut winner and the form of her narrow Listed win at Sandown (7f, good) reads all the better now; however, she went missing from July until finishing down the field at 28-1 in the Irish 1,000 Guineas, so there are concerns.
runners.comment: Unbeaten at two but only 9th at 28-1 in the Irish 1,000 Guineas after a long absence

runners.name: Passing Thought
runners.spotlight: 6f maiden winner last season on soft ground; easily her best form was encouragingly on her reappearance on good to firm at Wetherby (7f handicap) where she was in rear for the most part but led late on for a length success; further progress is by no means out of the question for yard that won this in 2018 and last year.
runners.comment: Career-best form to win at Wetherby latest and further progress is not out of the question

runners.name: Awaken
runners.spotlight: Has to rebound from a nothing run in the Nell Gwyn in April but perhaps it's better to judge her on 2yo form, which included a second to the brilliant Venetian Sun in the 6f Albany at this meeting and another fine run in a 7f Group 3; both those efforts were on good to firm and pedigree bodes well for this first step up to 1m; her profile isn't a million miles away from Soprano's who won this for the same connections two years ago; possibilities.
runners.comment: Chance if judged on her smart 2yo form and this step up in distance is worth exploring

runners.name: Harlequin Breeze
runners.spotlight: Close up behind Zooming in a novice race last season; ran well in her handicap (1m, good) on penultimate start but was only fourth and it was a small field she disposed of back in a novice at Leicester (good to firm; beat Crownright); it's difficult to envisage her being good enough.
runners.comment: Fourth on handicap debut before disposing of a small field back in a novice

runners.name: Tavana
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f winner last season but got her comeuppance when tried at Group 3 and Listed level; sister to a 1m winner but she was a fader when beating one home over this far on handicap debut at Newbury a month ago.
runners.comment: Dual 7f winner; needed to run a lot better on comeback (handicap debut) to be considered

runners.name: Spinning Lizzie
runners.spotlight: Wins at 6f (good) and 1m (AW); only midfield in a 7f Group 3 Guineas trial and then beaten 6.5l into third in the 1m2f Listed Pretty Polly; this trip might be a better fit but she's hardly on the crest of a wave right now.
runners.comment: Returning to 1m may help but she's hardly on the crest of a wave right now

runners.name: Al Rateel
runners.spotlight: French-trained filly whose first two races were on the AW, winning by 3l on debut before well beaten in a Listed race; raised her profile when third in the Italian 1,000 Guineas (good) and she came from further back than the winner Just Call Me Angel; this still looks a big ask on what she's shown thus far.
runners.comment: French-trained filly who was third in the Italian 1,000 Guineas; will need more again

runners.name: Causeway
runners.spotlight: Fourth on heavy on debut but marched through the ranks with wins in all his four starts since; the last two were on good going (unraced on firmer) at the Curragh where he asserted by 2l late on in a 1m Listed race and led well over 1f out when prevailing by a head in 1m2f Group 3; the cheekpieces worn on penultimate start are now restored; likely to stay 1m4f and has the best form; needs great respect coming from top stable and a top family.
runners.comment: Likely to stay and has the best form after Listed and 1m2f Group 3 wins last two starts
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Venetian Prince
runners.spotlight: Won on good to firm as 2yo; 80-1 when among the backmarkers in the 2,000 Guineas at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) in May in first-time cheekpieces; all change with blinkers, 1m3f and heavy going for the Derby Italiano at Milan 17 days ago and it worked a treat as he came home strongly from midfield to win in the final strides; that was a doughty performance and this is another Group 2, but he faces stronger opposition.
runners.comment: Came home strongly from midfield to win Derby Italiano (1m3f, heavy) in the final strides

runners.name: Water To Wine
runners.spotlight: By Kingman out of French 1m4f Group 2 winner; evens for debut in a maiden at Newbury (1m3f, good) in April and looked a superb prospect, striding just over 4l clear from Alderman who was fifth in the Derby on his next outing; this one's next race was meant to be the Chester Vase but he had a temperature, so connections had to settle for 11l romp from the front at 1-12 in a Kempton novice (1m3f, AW); he's not looked the finished article but it would seemingly be tough to find a 3yo who brings greater promise.
runners.comment: 2-2 at 1m3f in maiden/novice; it would be tough to find a 3yo who brings greater promise

runners.name: Ancient Egypt
runners.spotlight: First four starts were on good to firm and he registered an ultimately authoritative win in a five-runner Listed race at Newmarket (1m2f) in May, helping to make the running and with 1m4f looking a strong option; managing just eighth of 14 in the Derby at Epsom (1m4f, soft; 28-1) 13 days ago was therefore disappointing, but the earlier impression cannot yet be discarded.
runners.comment: Disappointing in the Derby (soft); earlier impression (1m2f, good to firm) not discarded

runners.name: Golden Story
runners.spotlight: Brother to 1m4f Great Voltigeur third Arabian Force; rather pulled himself up in front when winning his only start as 2yo (1m, soft); still green on reappearance at Chester (1m2f, good) behind the subsequent French Derby winner Constitution River, but he stuck to his task well and that was even more the hallmark of his extremely narrow, front-running win in four-runner Listed race at Goodwood (1m3f, good) four weeks ago; the runner-up last time was again beaten a nose in the 1m6f Group 2 here on Wednesday; this one is getting the hang of it and a much bigger performance could be on its way.
runners.comment: Getting the hang of it (1m3f Listed win) and a much bigger performance could be on its way

runners.name: Echo Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied first time and improved when off the mark in eight-runner novice at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago, leading over 2f out and comfortably seeing off yesterday's King George V last Blue Hercules; this son of Sea The Stars shaped as if 1m4f would be within reach, but he has a mountain to climb in this much higher class.
runners.comment: Has a mountain to climb even though he won a Chepstow novice (1m2f) comfortably last time

runners.name: Simplify
runners.spotlight: Hugely progressive this year, winning 5f handicaps at Wolverhampton and Thirsk before excelling herself when second to Dickensian in a Listed event at York (5f, good) five weeks ago; a 5lb rise isn't ideal but she's on the up and this strong-travelling filly appeals as the type to take well to the demands of this race.
runners.comment: Hugely progressive this year; latest Listed second another personal best; big run likely

runners.name: Ghost Mode
runners.spotlight: Useful 2yo and took his form to a new level with an emphatic win at Southwell (6f, AW) in March; a refusal to settle has compromised him in Group 3/Listed events on his last two starts but this mark is surely in range judged on that Southwell win and he is interesting now dropped to 5f for the first time; hooded every time except today.
runners.comment: Dazzling win at Southwell (6f) in March; pulled chance away twice since; interesting at 5f

runners.name: Comical Point
runners.spotlight: Fifth, beaten 4l, in last season's Norfolk Stakes over C&D (good to firm); tongue tied and has turned in three solid efforts in 2026, latterly when second of ten in a 6f handicap at Windsor (good to firm) 18 days ago when headed close home; worth another crack at 5f and likely to go well.
runners.comment: Three solid efforts in 2026 and dropping back to 5f could help; likely to run well

runners.name: Ipanema Queen
runners.spotlight: Won three sprints for Adrian Murray as a 2yo; back to her best when a close fourth of 12 in a handicap at the Curragh (5f, good to yielding) but even that effort lags short of what will be necessary to win.
runners.comment: Three 2yo wins; good fourth on handicap debut latest; this demands further progress
runners.owner: Stonestreet Stables, LLC & AMO Racing Limited

runners.name: Dazzling Haze
runners.spotlight: Three wins to her name, all on sharp tracks, the latest when making all at Thirsk (5f, good) 19 days ago; the combination of a 6lb rise, other pace and considerably stiffer opposition could leave her vulnerable.
runners.comment: Made all at Thirsk 19 days ago but up 6lb in a much stronger race today

runners.name: Calico Blue
runners.spotlight: He has improved his RPR with each of his four starts, bettering 2yo form when chasing home Black Star Boy at Sandown (5f, good) eight weeks ago; unexposed at the trip but that latest form has taken some knocks and he's up 2lb.
runners.comment: Ran well over 5f on reappearance but that form has taken some knocks

runners.name: Bacio
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; the winner of three of his four starts, the latest an impressive display at Churchill Downs (5f, firm) in April; trainer typically ebullient about his chance but his blistering early speed and ability to handle a bend like a greyhound, both huge assets in the US, will not be quite so advantageous on this stiff, straight track; that said, he has the stands' rail draw and looks sure to give his supporters a run for their money.
runners.comment: Speedy 3yo who is 3-4 in the US; the stiff, straight track may not play to his strengths

runners.name: May Angel
runners.spotlight: All six runs have been at 7f, winning an AW novice last summer; had wind and gelding operations prior to his reappearance at Newbury (good) in April and ran well for third; filled the same spot in a handicap here six weeks ago; improvement essential now tackling 5f in a red-hot handicap; tongue-tie enlisted.
runners.comment: Placed in two 7f handicaps off this mark in 2026; now drops to 5f with a tongue-tie fitted

runners.name: Dickensian
runners.spotlight: Finished second in the Windsor Castle over C&D at this meeting 12 months ago before third-place finishes in three more Group/Listed races; returned from eight months off to win a 5f Listed event at York five weeks ago, missing the break but avoiding the fierce early pace as a result and sweeping through late on; has a touch of class and stable sent out the topweight to finish second in this race 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Ran well at the meeting last year; Listed win on return; yard's topweight was 2nd in 2025
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Moojeed
runners.spotlight: Made all in a Group 3 at Chantilly (6f, good to soft) last June; didn't run badly behind two seasoned sprinters in the Group 3 Prix Du Gros-Chene back at Chantilly (5f, good) 19 days ago; fast ground will be a first for him but he still has his best days to come.
runners.comment: Lightly raced Group 3 winner; fast ground will be new but still has best days ahead of him

runners.name: Westport
runners.spotlight: Brings an unbeaten record into the race, successful on debut at Lingfield (6f, AW) and adding novice wins at Leicester (6f, good) and Hamilton (5f, good); beat three inferior rivals at odds of 1-18 at Hamilton and while his flawless record can't be knocked, this will be a very different test of his credentials.
runners.comment: 3-3 and still has potential; this is a very different test, however
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Miss Yechance
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in last year's Queen Mary when trained by Jack Morland; her best form in Dubai this winter came in her two 5f runs on turf, winning off 5lb lower in January; brings different formlines to most and she isn't a forlorn hope on her first run for Karl Burke.
runners.comment: Two 5f turf runs in Dubai this year read well; interesting on first run for Karl Burke

runners.name: Sirius A
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Leicester (7f, good to firm) on his second run as a 2yo; creditable efforts in Listed/Group 3 events afterwards (5f-7f); only had to match that form to win a 6f conditions event at Wolverhampton on his return in April and he was put in his place in Listed race at Newbury (6f, good) five weeks ago; Ryan Moore booked but this looks a tough mark and the return to 5f isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Useful 2yo and made a winning return on AW in April; well held latest; mark looks tough

runners.name: Sovereign Spell
runners.spotlight: Won 6f events at Wolverhampton (AW) and Newmarket (good) on his first two starts this year; progress stalled behind Ten Carat Harry last time; high in the weights back at 5f.
runners.comment: Won his first two starts this year but his progress stalled behind Ten Carat Harry latest

runners.name: Jazl
runners.spotlight: All runs over 6f on good to firm ground; beaten favourite in both starts as a 2yo, after which he was gelded; he has returned an improved performer in 2026, easily winning a Windsor maiden before producing a dominant display in a Leicester handicap 25 days ago; strong at the finish despite racing freely in the early stages on that latest occasion and he looks plenty quick enough for 5f; a 10lb hike is no gift but the Leicester form looks strong and he could well be a Group horse in the making; stable won this last year; lots to like.
runners.comment: 2-2 since gelded; created big impression at Leicester last month; 5f should be fine; solid

runners.name: Sandal's Song
runners.spotlight: Finished third in last season's Norfolk Stakes over C&D (good to firm) and it's easy enough to make a handicapping case for him on that run; two defeats in the USA this spring, finishing second to a useful sort at Churchill Downs six weeks ago; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Good chance judged on last season's Norfolk Stakes third; two defeats in USA this spring

runners.name: Starmade
runners.spotlight: Placed in his three turf runs but his wins have come on the two occasions he has tackled the Tapeta at Newcastle; smooth win over 6f seven weeks ago but he is in much deeper today.
runners.comment: 2-2 on AW but has shown promise on turf; needs much more upped in class

runners.name: Revival Power
runners.spotlight: Represents the same connections as 2020 winner Art Power; 3-6 as a 2yo, including the Group 2 Flying Childers at Doncaster (5f, good); dropped away to finish seventh of ten behind Dickensian at York (5f, good) on her reappearance having forced a strong pace; should be sharper today but this will be a tough race to dominate; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Group 2 win last year; may have needed York return; connections won this race in 2020

runners.name: Cherry Baker
runners.spotlight: A win and a second at Chester (both 6f, good) in her two handicap runs; going the right way but she has a different set of conditions to negotiate today.
runners.comment: 1st/2nd in two handicap runs, both over 6f on good ground; now down to 5f on faster ground

runners.name: Gold Digger
runners.spotlight: All runs over 6f on good to firm; solid form when making a winning debut at Yarmouth (well backed) last May; sixth in a strong Albany Stakes at this meeting the following month; absent for 346 days prior to capitalising on a good opportunity at Windsor 18 days ago; has room for improvement at the start and wouldn't want to concede much early ground now dropped to 5f; she does have the potential to be better than this mark though.
runners.comment: Unexposed and promising sprinter; ran well at this meeting in 2025; first run at 5f

runners.name: Tricky Tel
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Chester as a 2yo; on the up this season, running well from a poor draw at Chester (6f, good) on his reappearance before winning at Hamilton (6f, good to firm) three weeks later; took his form to a new level when beating older horses at Chester (5f, good to soft) and the manner of that success offered hope of further progress; 1lb wrong under his penalty but horses arriving on the back of a recent win have fared well in this race (Latin Lover won, Dream Composer and Radio Goo Goo both placed) and Toby Moore takes off 7lb; central draw perhaps not ideal but plenty to like otherwise.
runners.comment: Arrives on a sharp upward curve; big player despite penalty for last week's Chester win

runners.name: Stargazed
runners.spotlight: 5f win at Ripon (good to soft) as a 2yo; took a good step forward when second of 17 on her seasonal/handicap debut at York (5f, good) five weeks ago, impressing with how she travelled through the race, and a 3lb rise is fair enough; bred to be a Group horse and she's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Fine sprinting pedigree; shaped well at York on seasonal debut; unexposed and high on list

runners.name: Black Star Boy
runners.spotlight: Proved well suited to a stiff 5f when strong at the finish at Sandown on his return in April; that form has taken some knocks though and he was behind some of today's rivals at York last month.
runners.comment: Improved to win at Sandown in April but that form has taken some knocks

runners.name: Ten Carat Harry
runners.spotlight: Made hay on AW over the winter and then found another burst of improvement to win at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last month; admirable as he is, this looks an extremely stiff task now dropping to 5f for the first time; sold for £200,000 at the Goffs London Sale on Monday.
runners.comment: Admirable campaigner but looks high in the weights now dropping to 5f for the first time

runners.name: Exclamation
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in the Norfolk Stakes here last year; game front-running effort to win at the May meeting at Chester (5f, good) but well beaten in Dickensian's York race one week later; the addition of cheekpieces isn't enough to tempt in this field.
runners.comment: Sprung a surprise with game Chester win last month; behind a few of these at York since

runners.name: Baker Blue
runners.spotlight: Won twice on Tapeta over the winter, notably a runaway defeat of Fortification (who is now 8lb higher) at Wolverhampton in February; down the field in a Listed event at Chantilly (5.5f, heavy) in March; unlikely to dominate this field and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Impressed at Wolverhampton in February; 9lb higher and returning from three months off
runners.owner: Ms L Mars & Mrs A Brereton

runners.name: Gentle George
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Sandown (5f, soft) and a novice at Haydock (6f, good to soft) for Mick Appleby as a 2yo; well-beaten 50-1 shot on stable debut at Chester (7.6f, good) last month; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Well-beaten 50-1 shot on last month's stable debut; plenty to prove

runners.name: Zelaina
runners.spotlight: Highly tried after her impressive debut success at Nottingham (5f, good) last June and she came up short; fresh may be the time to catch her but Karl Burke may have stronger candidates elsewhere.
runners.comment: Impressed on 2yo debut but came up shy in better company afterwards; trainer runs three

races.race_name: ABP Ports Of Grimsby & Immingham Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Getaway Vic
runners.spotlight: Dual bumper winner who has shaped with promise in his two runs over hurdles, the latest when runner-up at Cartmel (2m6f, good) last month in first-time cheekpieces (left off here); steps up in trip and looks sure to go close.
runners.comment: Dual bumper winner who has shaped with promise in his two runs over hurdles.

runners.name: Royal Rocket
runners.spotlight: Is slowly getting the hang of things over hurdles, most recently when runner-up at Warwick (2m3f, good) last month; steps back up in trip and holds place claims at least.
runners.comment: Is slowly getting the hang of things over hurdles, latest when runner-up at Warwick.

runners.name: Kiwi de Cotte
runners.spotlight: Dual point winner in Ireland but was pulled up on his rules debut in a novice hunter chase at Wexford (2m7f, good to yielding) last month; purchased for £15,000 by new connections subsequently and makes hurdles debut for champion trainer; fascinating contender and market will be best guide.
runners.comment: Pulled up in Irish hunter chase last month but now with Dan Skelton; market will guide

runners.name: Grey Gamble
runners.spotlight: Beaten 50l and 59l on her two runs over hurdles; steps up in trip but needs to show significantly more; stable also runs Royal Rocket.
runners.comment: Beaten 50l and 59l on her two runs over hurdles; steps up in trip.

runners.name: Premier Fantasy
runners.spotlight: Multiple point winner who made an encouraging start over hurdles when runner-up at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) 16 days ago; should be suited by this step up in trip and should run well.
runners.comment: Made an encouraging start over hurdles when runner-up at Newton Abbot 16 days ago.

runners.name: Saint Arion
runners.spotlight: First sign of ability when fourth of nine on hurdles debut at Worcester (2m, good) four weeks ago; takes a significant step up in trip and is worth keeping an eye on.
runners.comment: First sign of ability when fourth of nine on hurdles debut at Worcester four weeks ago.

runners.name: Lunar Morning
runners.spotlight: Has shown little in his two runs over hurdles; wears first-time visor and steps up in trip; easy to dismiss.
runners.comment: Has shown little in his two runs over hurdles; steps up in trip.

runners.name: Muskerry Rock
runners.spotlight: Every chance but couldn't quite get past when suffering an odds-on defeat on this month's Fontwell stable debut (3m2f), though did at least record a clear personal best good-ground effort there; 4lb well in as a result, but today's contest has a little bit more depth to it for the grade.
runners.comment: 4lb well in for last week's near miss in a slightly easier-looking Fontwell affair (3m2f).

runners.name: Jungle Wood
runners.spotlight: Still 4lb above the mark for last July's Worcester win (2m7f, good) without progressing further; 19l behind Cinderello on recent Fontwell return, and have to hope cheekpieces help tease out his resources longer.
runners.comment: 19l behind Cinderello on recent return; have to hope he'll last out longer in cheekpieces.

runners.name: Cinderello
runners.spotlight: 2-2 under Jack Hogan since returning from a seven-month break, keeping on well for Warwick (3m1f) and Fontwell (3m2f) good-ground successes; latest 6lb rise demands more, but she's progressing at a similar rate.
runners.comment: Warwick (3m1f) and Fontwell (3m2f) good-ground wins last month; up 6lb but progressing.

runners.name: Moro Rock
runners.spotlight: Just 1-21 overall but is back on the mark he defied at Stratford last July (3m3f, good), and he'd only just missed out in this corresponding race immediately beforehand (C&D record reads 2225); not out of this.
runners.comment: Back on his one winning mark; C&D record of 2225 includes 1l second in this last year.

runners.name: Time Was
runners.spotlight: Consistent without winning last autumn and winter, but checked out tamely behind Lady Fortune on last month's return here (2m6f, good); mixed record of seeing out stayers' trips, an Irish point win notwithstanding.
runners.comment: Consistent without winning last autumn/winter; pulled up behind Lady Fortune here latest.

runners.name: Matoury
runners.spotlight: Occasionally inclined to put his head in the air, but recorded a 2m5f win here off 1lb lower this time a year ago (good) and last month's comeback third was among his better runs since; does stay this far.
runners.comment: 2m5f winner here off 1lb lower a year ago (good); solid recent return; can stay this far.

runners.name: Reforme
runners.spotlight: Consistent over fences last autumn and winter; struggled for fluency on both runs since a break, pulling up behind Cinderello latest.
runners.comment: Consistent win or lose late last year, but jumping issues have compromised him latterly.

runners.name: Klervia
runners.spotlight: Ever more temptingly handicapped, and still doing some okay late work behind the uncontainable Beorma over Southwell's 2m4f last time; previous tribulations in two Cornish points and one other staying hurdle temper enthusiasm ahead of this step back up in trip, however.
runners.comment: Temptingly handicapped, and fair third latest; unconvinced she needs a stayers' trip now.

runners.name: Lady Fortune
runners.spotlight: Either or both of the wind surgery and headgear tweak inspired her to dig in for a head victory here latest (2m6f, good; Time Was pulled up); need to hope that improvement can translate to longer trips also, but clearly far enough down the weights to make hay if a corner has been turned.
runners.comment: Up 4lb for breakthrough win here latest (2m6f, good); chance if longer trip is no issue.

runners.name: Braganza Bay
runners.spotlight: All five career wins gained in Huntingdon or Hereford staying handicaps (one chase); cashed in a career-low mark to the tune of 11l at the former venue latest (good), to which the assessor's reaction has been swift and punitive; back to requiring a likely personal best to follow up.
runners.comment: Cashed in a career-low mark at Huntingdon latest (3m1f); assessor quick to respond.

runners.name: Thickthorn Tom
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fourth attempt over fences at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) last month; 6lb higher here but looks sure to figure with that win under his belt.
runners.comment: Off the mark at the fourth attempt over fences at Newton Abbot last month.

runners.name: Wings On Wheels
runners.spotlight: 0-7 under rules but ran his best race over fences when runner-up at Worcester (2m4f, good) eight days ago in first-time visor (retained) when attempting to make all; 3lb higher in future chases and should run well.
runners.comment: Ran his best race over fences when runner-up at Worcester eight days ago.

runners.name: Getaway Jewel
runners.spotlight: All five wins have been over 2m-2m1f, two of which came at this track in 2022; made a pleasing return after a five-month break when runner-up at Fakenham (2m, good) last month but may be vulnerable to younger rivals here.
runners.comment: Made a pleasing return after a five-month break when runner-up at Fakenham last month.

runners.name: Arctic Saint
runners.spotlight: 0-13 under rules but made a bright start to chasing when runner-up at Sedgefield (2m5f, good) in April following his third wind op; absence since is a slight concern, but otherwise he should not be far away.
runners.comment: 0-13 under rules but made a bright start to chasing when runner-up at Sedgefield in April.

runners.name: Chateau Du Lys
runners.spotlight: Encouraging second at this track (2m1f, good) in April in first-time cheekpieces (retained since) but has been tailed off at Huntingdon and Fakenham since; all of a sudden has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Second at this track in April but has been tailed off at Huntingdon and Fakenham since.

runners.name: Shes All Pride
runners.spotlight: Has shown bits of form over hurdles but needs improvement on her chasing debut.
runners.comment: Has shown bits of form over hurdles but needs improvement on her chasing debut.

races.race_name: Global Shipping Services Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Beorma
runners.spotlight: Effectively runs off 3lb lower here than when last week's chase success in a Newton Abbot match (2m, good) completed her six-timer inside 15 days; unraced right-handed so far during her winning streak, but no real reason why that or the return to hurdling should present a problem; 10lb well in.
runners.comment: 6-6 since the end of May; 10lb well in after last week's three chase wins (2m-2m4f, good).

runners.name: Hello Sweety
runners.spotlight: Free-going mare whose latest game, dominating Fakenham success took her record to 3-5 in 2m contests there; 3lb rise doesn't look prohibitive, but a best RPR of 81 going right-handed versus 104 anticlockwise tempers greater confidence.
runners.comment: 3-5 around Fakenham following latest win (2m, good); still to convince as much clockwise.

runners.name: The Secret Pearl
runners.spotlight: C&D mares' maiden winner in April 2025 (good) on sole previous visit, and mixed signals since as to whether she's as good over further; some appeal granted the lowest mark she's ever raced off in a handicap.
runners.comment: Some appeal returned to the C&D of her sole previous win (mares' maiden) in April 2025.

runners.name: Miss Friday Lions
runners.spotlight: Back to near her best when a near-5l third at Kelso latest (2m, good); never getting there quickly enough, but that form is at least working out with the first and fourth winners next time; respected.
runners.comment: Never getting there in time at Kelso latest, but that form has been boosted twice since.

runners.name: Kottayam
runners.spotlight: Bits of placed form in good-ground mares' maidens (2m/2m4f) for Patrick Neville last summer, but has badly lost her way since; returning tongue-tie and newly fitted cheekpieces aren't persuasive.
runners.comment: Lost her way since some okay placed form in mares' maidens (2m/2m4f) last summer.

runners.name: Little Soiree
runners.spotlight: Huntingdon mares' handicap winner (2m, good) on penultimate start for Chris Gordon just over a year ago, but basically regressive since; 3lb out of the weights this time and not hard to pass over.
runners.comment: May 2025 winner for Chris Gordon (2m, good) but regressive since; 3lb wrong today.

runners.name: Assorda
runners.spotlight: Not threatened to supplement March 2025's lucky Catterick novice success (2m, good) in five starts since almost a year off, including over this C&D latest; tongue-tie returns.
runners.comment: Lucky Catterick winner in March 2025 (2m, good); not threatened further gains since.

runners.name: Miracles Do Happen
runners.spotlight: More tractable than often the case when obliging at Taunton in April (2m1f, good); signed off for Tom Lacey on a low note after, and mark still 5lb above that winning one has no wriggle room about it.
runners.comment: Mark still 5lb above April's winning one (2m1f, good) has no wriggle room; stable debut.

races.race_name: Associated British Ports "Summer Plate Trial" Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Seaview Rock
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on his second start over hurdles at Leicester (1m7f, heavy) in January for Olly Murphy; promising second on chase debut at Southwell (2m4f, good) last month and subsequently sold for £30,000 to join new connections; third of five at Southwell (2m, good) 17 days ago on stable debut and will be more at home over this longer distance; lightly raced and there may be better to come.
runners.comment: Third at Southwell 17 days ago on stable debut; steps up in trip.

runners.name: Bhaloo
runners.spotlight: Dual winning chaser who geared up for a summer campaign when runner-up at Uttoxeter (3m, good) last month; third in the Summer Plate over C&D last summer and connections will be hopeful of a good run here before his main target.
runners.comment: Geared up for a summer campaign when runner-up at Uttoxeter last month.

runners.name: Genois
runners.spotlight: Came from well off the pace to land his third win over fences in a veterans' handicap at Exeter (2m3f, good to soft) in March off 5lb lower; runner-up in a similar contest at Haydock (2m4f, good) the following month but was pulled up after making several jumping errors at Aintree last time; may be a shade high in the weights at present.
runners.comment: Won a veterans' handicap at Exeter in March and second in a similar contest next time.

runners.name: Bleu d'Enfer
runners.spotlight: Has a modest strike rate of 2-30 but ran his best race over fences when beaten a neck at Aintree (2m4f, good) five weeks ago (with Genois pulled up); holds claims if able to build on that run off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Ran his best race over fences when beaten a neck at Aintree five weeks ago.

runners.name: Mahons Glory
runners.spotlight: Capitalized on a drop in grade to land his third win over fences at Huntingdon (2m4f, good to soft) in March off 6lb lower; ran respectably when sixth in the Topham over the National fences, and when fourth in the Oaksey Chase at Sandown in April; sure to run well but vulnerable off top weight.
runners.comment: Landed his third win over fences at Huntingdon in March off 6lb lower.
runners.owner: C Nightingale & Hold My Beer Partnership

runners.name: Gateau De Miel
runners.spotlight: Recorded his third chase success on his stable debut at Stratford (2m5f, good to firm) last month; that looked a weak affair and he has been hit by a 7lb rise but he may still have more in his locker for his new yard.
runners.comment: Recorded his third chase success on his stable debut at Stratford last month.

runners.name: Ostend
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over fences at this track (2m3f, good) in March 2025 for Alastair Ralph but has been below that form in four runs since; sold for £4,000 to join current yard last month and is well handicapped if he can put his best foot forward on his stable debut.
runners.comment: Won at this track in March 2025 but below form since.

races.race_name: ABP Ports Of Hull & Goole Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Bunker Bay
runners.spotlight: Improved RPRs with each successive visit to Market Rasen, culminating in good-ground 2m1f and C&D wins in the past three weeks; unpenalised for an 11l romp in the latter, and he'll rarely have a better opportunity to round off a hat-trick.
runners.comment: Wins over 2m1f and C&D (both good) of late, the latter off today's mark; 7lb well in.

runners.name: Onehundredneighty
runners.spotlight: The one for money on last month's stable debut at Warwick, but found it happening too quickly dropped back to 2m; career-low mark unchanged, and likely to get the combination of 2m4f and a sound surface for the first time if the forecast is accurate.
runners.comment: Well backed but outpaced dropped to 2m on last month's stable debut; remains of interest.

runners.name: Midnight Pass
runners.spotlight: Never travelling when pulled up at Hereford last month, and no more encouragement returned to novice company since; new career-low mark still insufficiently tempting.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Hereford last month; no nearer a win in a novice since.

runners.name: Mancero
runners.spotlight: First success since his French debut when winning going away at Worcester last month (2m4f, good), albeit needing getting after on the way round; new 6lb higher mark is some way in excess of any RPR ever run to.
runners.comment: 6lb rise for last-time Worcester win (2m4f) places mark well above any RPR ever run to.

runners.name: Lone Star
runners.spotlight: Prolific in 2022-23 but just one win in her last 29 starts, gained over this C&D but off 5lb lower; mostly consistent without threatening this year, and minor honours likeliest at best.
runners.comment: Sole win in 29 most recent starts came over this C&D last year; minor claims at best.

runners.name: Vivid Pink
runners.spotlight: No match for Gms Prince here in September, but Huntingdon victory on sole start after (also 2m3f, good; placed horses both won next time) rates a major personal best; respected.
runners.comment: Signed off last autumn with personal best in Huntingdon win (2m3f; good); respected.

runners.name: Tuba
runners.spotlight: Carried the good cheer of a successful spell on the Tapeta back across to hurdles at Fakenham latest (3m, good), winning as he pleased; 8lb rise looks about right, and has stamina to spare down in trip.
runners.comment: Carried Tapeta form back across to hurdling with Fakenham win latest; 8lb rise is fair.

runners.name: Red Trail
runners.spotlight: 0-16 over hurdles; over 15l to find with Bunker Bay on their clash here last month (2m1f) and well below that level at Southwell since.
runners.comment: 0-16 over hurdles; over 15l to find with Bunker Bay on their 2m1f clash here last month.

runners.name: Earlofbrackenstown
runners.spotlight: Only once within fewer than 20l of the winner when completing, and checked out quickly once headed on last month's stable debut.
runners.comment: Completing has been an issue in the past year, including on his stable debut latest.

runners.name: Gms Prince
