runners.spotlight: Just 1-26 over hurdles, and consistency since last September's 2m3f success here (good) has kept him 7lb above that winning mark; vulnerable to improvers but stronger podium claims than most.
runners.comment: 2m3f winner here last September (good) and still 7lb higher; ought to run his race again.

runners.name: Bargain Hunter
runners.spotlight: Beaten 52l and upwards in his qualifying starts (2m); longer trip a theoretical plus, but wins little until hurdling more fluently.
runners.comment: Well out the back on all three qualifying starts (2m); could ideally jump more fluently.

races.race_name: Humber Ports' Port Of Choice Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Scotto
runners.spotlight: Looked an exciting recruit when making a winning debut at Plumpton (2m2f, good) last month; it is often difficult to follow up under a penalty but he may be one against the trend and looks one to follow.
runners.comment: Looked an exciting recruit when making a winning debut at Plumpton last month.

runners.name: Fine Thing
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros 3yo; seventh foal; half-sister to point and 2m3f/3m hurdle winner Ree Okka; dam unraced half-sister to high-class 2m-3m hurdle/chase winner Identity Thief and useful 2m/2m7f hurdle winner Oscarteea; best of four runs in Irish points when runner-up last month, shortly before a £20,000 sale; contender on debut for top yard.
runners.comment: Best of four runs in Irish points when runner-up last month;  contender for top yard.

runners.name: Missy Moises
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam French 2m1f hurdle winner, sister to useful 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Cruz Control, out of French 2m5f Listed chase-winning half-sister to top-class 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Voy Por Ustedes; that reads well but market will be the best guide.
runners.comment: Related to some notable winners but market will be best guide.

runners.name: Royalty Row'd
runners.spotlight: Made an encouraging debut when third of seven finishers on his debut at Perth (2m, good to soft) last month; looks a nice prospect who should win races when he tackles hurdles.
runners.comment: Made an encouraging debut when third on his debut at Perth last month.

runners.name: Master Chuggy
runners.spotlight: 24,000euros foals; sixth foal; closely related to point winner Fortuitous Find, half-brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Prussia With Love; dam unraced sister to 2m2f hurdle/2m5f chase winner Kings Lodge, out of sister to high-class staying hurdler Marello; has shown ability in a couple of points and is an interesting prospect for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Has shown ability in a couple of points and is an interesting prospect for in-form yard.

runners.name: Masterius
runners.spotlight: 9,000euros 3yo; third foal; half-brother to winners Lowry (1m4f AQPS Flat/2m3f chase) and Jiang Li (1m4f AQPS Flat); dam unraced, from family of high-class 2m-3m hurdle/chase winners Envoi Allen (also Champion Bumper) and Eldorado Allen; runner-up in an Irish point last month and sold for £30,000 soon afterwards; an exciting prospect for yard that knows the time of day.
runners.comment: Runner-up in an Irish point last month and an exciting prospect for good yard.

races.race_name: In Memory Of Peter & Shirley Fryett Handicap

runners.name: Dancing With Drums
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come in AW handicaps over 6f, the latest at Kempton last month; hasn't shone in his previous attempts beyond that trip, but looked worth another go when third of 12 at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) last time; unexposed on turf and respected.
runners.comment: Looked worth another go over this far when third at Chepstow last time; respected

runners.name: Reginald Charles
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come on AW and he is 0-17 on turf, though he has been placed in both visits here including when beaten just a short head over this trip last October; each-way claims again.
runners.comment: 0-17 on turf but has been placed in both visits here; each-way claims again

runners.name: Valentine Catcher
runners.spotlight: Course winner (6f) who has been running well in defeat lately having fallen down the weights, as when third of nine at Carlisle (7f, good) three weeks ago; losing run now up to 16, though.
runners.comment: Running well in defeat lately having fallen down the weights, but losing run now up to 16

runners.name: Mecca's Symphony
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but she responded well to the tongue-tie when beaten 2l into second of 11 behind Mayo County over C&D (good to firm) last month; 5lb better off with the winner and a player.
runners.comment: 0-8; finished 2l runner-up to Mayo County over C&D last month and 5lb better off; a player

runners.name: Looks Fantastic
runners.spotlight: Sole success came over C&D (good to firm) in April, but he isn't very consistent and ran particularly poorly at Leicester (6f, good to firm) last time; has questions to answer.
runners.comment: C&D winner but isn't very consistent and ran particularly poorly last time

runners.name: Mayo County
runners.spotlight: Mostly running well since late last year, including a second success over this C&D (good to firm) last month; ran with credit when fourth of 11 at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) 13 days ago and high on the list back here, especially if the ground continues to dry out.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner including last month; ran with credit last time; high on list back here

runners.name: Travis
runners.spotlight: Formerly consistent and has dropped to a handy mark on the pick of his form, but heavy defeats over 1m here and at Ripon in his last two starts leave him with enough to prove.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats in his last two starts leave him with enough to prove

runners.name: Super Hit
runners.spotlight: Losing run up to 19, but hasn't been with James Owen for long; ran well in his first two starts for the yard before pulling his chance away at Bath four weeks ago; dropping back to 7f may be no bad thing so worth a second look in the returning hood.
runners.comment: Dropping back to 7f may be no had thing so worth a second look in the returning hood

runners.name: Operation Gimcrack
runners.spotlight: Both wins came over this trip on the AW at the start of the year, but he has made the frame several times on turf; was found to be coughing after running poorly at Thirsk ten weeks ago; can go well fresh and not easily dismissed.
runners.comment: Was found to be coughing last time; can go well fresh and not easily dismissed

runners.name: Palmarian
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all 11 starts including five for this yard, but continues to edge down the weights and endured a bit of a nightmare at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) 13 days ago; shapes as though he has a bigger effort in him and worth a second look.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all 11 starts but shapes as though he has a bigger effort in him
runners.owner: MDP Racing And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Raja Charger
runners.spotlight: Sent off three-figure prices for all five starts and has failed to beat a rival; switch to blinkers needs to have a dramatic effect.
runners.comment: Has failed to beat a rival in all five starts; blinkers on

runners.name: Jesmond Dawn
runners.spotlight: Course win came over 6f, but he stays this far and further; only just failed to last home on his return from four months off at Ripon (1m, good) 16 days ago and one for the shortlist despite a 2lb rise.
runners.comment: Only just failed to last home at Ripon 16 days ago; shortlisted despite 2lb rise

runners.name: Stoneywell
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden whose best effort came when third of seven in a Leicester novice (7f, good; 80-1) last October; that form is dubious, however. and he hasn't matched it since; best watched despite another 3lb drop.
runners.comment: Form of his third at Leicester last October is dubious and he hasn't matched it since

runners.name: Front Gunner
runners.spotlight: Sole turf win came off 2lb lower in this race last year, when with Ann Duffield, and he is 1lb below his last winning mark; interesting to see how he fares in the market on his first start for Ivan Furtado (same owners).
runners.comment: Won this race last year; interesting to see how he fares in the market on stable debut

races.race_name: Free Racing TV At racingtv.com/freetrial Handicap

runners.name: Harswell River
runners.spotlight: Exposed after 20 starts but she does have a good record in handicaps here (1323); no reason why she shouldn't make her presence felt again.
runners.comment: Has a good record in handicaps here; no reason why she shouldn't make her presence felt

runners.name: Hey Havana
runners.spotlight: Only beaten half a length on stable debut over C&D (good) last month on his return from 192 days off and not disgraced when fifth of 13 in a slightly better race at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) on Monday; interesting back here with cheekpieces given a go.
runners.comment: Just beaten on return over C&D last month; ran well at Wetherby on Monday; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Case Study
runners.spotlight: Responded well to cheekpieces when second of ten at Lingfield (1m, AW) 15 days ago; a player if transferring the form back to turf.
runners.comment: Second of ten at Lingfield (AW) 15 days ago; player if transferring the form back to turf

runners.name: Ten Sixty Six
runners.spotlight: Placed in her first three runs in 7f turf maidens last August, but has gone the wrong way in three outings since including both handicaps; drops in grade, but still has something to prove.
runners.comment: Has gone the wrong way in last three starts; drops in grade but still something to prove

runners.name: Twilight Dawn
runners.spotlight: Ran her best race so far when third of nine over C&D (good) last month; races off the same mark and a similar effort would put her firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Finished third over C&D last month; a similar effort would put her firmly in the mix

runners.name: Secret Testimony
runners.spotlight: 50-1 when narrowly landing a Newcastle nursery (7f, AW) in November, but not matched that form in three starts since returning in April, including when attracting market support over C&D last month.
runners.comment: Surprise nursery winner at Newcastle in November but not matched that form since

runners.name: What Fools Believe
runners.spotlight: Much her best effort when narrowly winning a handicap at Southwell (1m, AW) in April but well held in all three starts since including two on turf; cheekpieces return; stable won this last year.
runners.comment: Well held in three starts since winning at Southwell; stable took this last year

runners.name: Enduring Story
runners.spotlight: Only 2l behind Twilight Dawn when fifth of nine over C&D (good) on last month's handicap debut but the rest of her form makes her hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Fair effort over C&D on her penultimate start but still hard to fancy

runners.name: Only Dream Big
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all seven starts, though encouraging that her best effort was when beaten less than 2l into fourth of ten on her reappearance over C&D last month; now 1lb lower; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Best effort when fourth of ten on her reappearance over C&D last month; now 1lb lower

runners.name: Itszaboy
runners.spotlight: All four starts have been here and was well beaten each time, the latest on his reappearance/handicap debut (1m, good to firm) in April; best watched.
runners.comment: All four starts have been here and was well beaten each time; best watched

races.race_name: Marske Fabrications & Engineering Handicap (Straight Mile Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Classic Encounter
runners.spotlight: Respectable effort when fourth of 12 at Newbury (1m, good to firm) this month and the return here makes him especially interesting given he is 2-2 over C&D; drying ground a positive.
runners.comment: Return here makes him interesting given he is 2-2 over C&D; drying ground a positive

runners.name: City Of Poets
runners.spotlight: 3-9; didn't enjoy much luck when sent off one of the market leaders for the Thirsk Hunt Cup (1m, good to firm) last month, but ran his race when beaten just over a length into fourth of 13 over the same C&D (good) a fortnight later; one for the shortlist down another 1lb.
runners.comment: 3-9; ran well to finish fourth at Thirsk last time; one for the shortlist down another 1lb

runners.name: Double Parked
runners.spotlight: Found 1m2f too far at York five weeks ago but the return here is a positive given his course record (111373); still 3lb above his last winning mark but holds each-way claims with a tongue-tie given a go.
runners.comment: Course record reads 111373; holds each-way claims; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Mehmas Champion
runners.spotlight: Showed some good form in Germany early last year including when fourth in their 2,000 Guineas (1m, good; Group 2), but quiet in both runs since joining this stable after almost a year off; best watched on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Quiet in both runs since joining this stable; best watched on handicap debut

runners.name: New Image
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and mainly disappointing since his last success in August 2024, the exception being when beaten under a length into third of nine on his reappearance over C&D (good to firm) in April; now 3lb lower but he has become hard to predict.
runners.comment: 3lb lower than when a close third over C&D in April but has become hard to predict

runners.name: Fiery Damsel
runners.spotlight: Improved in three starts over 6f on the AW in the autumn, winning novices at Kempton and Southwell; still something of an unknown quantity now making her handicap/turf debut after eight months off, but her pedigree suggests the extra 2f may not be an issue; market helpful.
runners.comment: Won two AW novices last autumn; handicap/turf debut after eight months off; watch market

runners.name: Theoryofeverything
runners.spotlight: Has shown signs of a resurgence and is back down to 0-90 company for the first time since successful at Ayr (1m) last July, but all four wins have come on soft ground so drying conditions wouldn't be ideal; stable triple-handed.
runners.comment: Return to a 0-90 is a positive but drying conditions wouldn't be ideal

runners.name: Nikovo
runners.spotlight: Rated much lower on turf (2-20) than on the AW (5-22); hasn't run at all badly in his last two starts on grass including when eighth of 28 in the Victoria Cup; each-way claims down another 2lb.
runners.comment: Hasn't run at all badly in his last two starts on turf; each-way claims down another 2lb

runners.name: Empirestateofmind
runners.spotlight: Has dropped 8lb lower than when last winning a handicap for good reason; his last two starts didn't suggest a return to winning ways was imminent.
runners.comment: Has dropped 8lb lower than when last winning a handicap but for good reason

runners.name: Milteye
runners.spotlight: Has mainly proved consistent since joining this yard (233213126101), his latest win coming by a head at Ripon (1m, good to soft) in April; probably needs a bit more back up in grade and may not want the ground to dry out too much.
runners.comment: Has mainly proved consistent since joining this yard but probably needs a bit more
runners.owner: Rise One Racing And W A Bethell

runners.name: Regalian
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut at Newcastle (7f, AW) early last year and placed over this trip and further in four of his six subsequent starts; has since left William Haggas for 16,000gns and the market should indicate what is expected on stable debut after 250 days off; cheekpieces given a go.
runners.comment: Makes his stable debut in first-time cheekpieces after 250 days off; watch market

races.race_name: Try Racing TV For Free Now Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Raggaas
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns yearling; kept on steadily in the tongue-tie (retained) when fourth of 11 on his Wolverhampton debut (8.6f, AW) this month; any improvement on that would make him a big player.
runners.comment: Kept on steadily when fourth on his Wolverhampton debut this month; big player

runners.name: New Poetic
runners.spotlight: 195,000gns yearling; beaten a fair way in two 1m soft-ground maidens in Ireland for Jessica Harrington last autumn, but has been found an uncompetitive race for his stable debut (same owner) after 229 days off, having been gelded since last seen; the longer trip should suit on breeding and he's respected.
runners.comment: Has been found an uncompetitive race for his stable debut; the longer trip should suit

runners.name: Shoof Baeed
runners.spotlight: 68,000gns yearling; brother to 8.6f AW 2yo winner Baba Ghanouj (RPR 86), closely related to 1m 2yo winner Star Caliber (94), half-brother to five winners incliding Agrotera (1m including AW Listed; 106); dam 6f 2yo winner (92); enough to like on pedigree and market support would be significant.
runners.comment: Enough to like on pedigree and market support would be significant

runners.name: He's Perfect
runners.spotlight: Sent off 12-1 when tailed off on last month's Kempton debut (7f, AW); needs the extra 3f and switch to turf to make a big difference; stable also runs Traveling Man.
runners.comment: Tailed off on last month's Kempton debut; needs the extra 3f to make a big difference

runners.name: The Resdev Willow
runners.spotlight: 66-1 when a tailed-off last of ten on last month's debut here (1m, good to firm).
runners.comment: 66-1 when a tailed-off last of ten on last month's debut over 1m here

runners.name: Traveling Man
runners.spotlight: Well-bred 360,000gns yearling; beaten a long way on his Salisbury debut (1m2f, soft; 8-1) ten days ago after blowing the start and running green; probably capable of better but comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: Blew the start and ran green on his Salisbury debut; comes with risks attached
runners.owner: P Hickman And Lord Lloyd Webber

races.race_name: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Room Fourteen
runners.spotlight: Has continued to progress since switching to turf handicaps with a hood fitted, following up her narrow Yarmouth success (1m2f, good to firm) in April with a second of nine at Ascot (1m2f, good to firm) last month (met traffic); subsequent wins by the first and fourth give the Ascot form substance and she is 2lb well in; holds major claims.
runners.comment: Continued to progress since switching to turf handicaps with a hood fitted; major claims

runners.name: Guesstimate
runners.spotlight: Placed in four of her five starts in maiden/novice company, the latest when stepped up to this trip at Goodwood (good) last month; needs to find a bit more on handicap debut, though.
runners.comment: Placed in four of her five starts, but needs to find a bit more on handicap debut

runners.name: Stoneacre Donny
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of a novice at Thirsk (7f, soft) last September and has been placed in all four handicaps since; may have finished closer with a clearer passage when third of ten at York (1m2f, good) last time; another big run seems likely back down in grade.
runners.comment: Has been placed in all four handicaps; another big run seems likely back down in grade

runners.name: Horu Kanu
runners.spotlight: 0-7; placed in two of his first three starts over 7f/1m since returning in April, but with cheekpieces fitted was well held at Carlisle (7.7f, good to firm) last time; mixed messages as regard the longer trip and he needs to find more.
runners.comment: 0-7; mixed messages as regard the longer trip and he needs to find more

runners.name: Raspoutine
runners.spotlight: Has improved since handicapping, including an easy win on his Lingfield reappearance (1m, AW) in April; has shown a tendency to edge left, though, and whether he is as effective on turf is a moot point; cheekpieces replace blinkers.
runners.comment: Has improved in handicaps, but whether he is better on AW is a moot point; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Lightening Lad
runners.spotlight: Has regressed in two starts since finishing second of ten on last November's Newcastle debut (7f, AW); needs a resurgence on handicap debut, but the longer trip should suit on breeding.
runners.comment: Has regressed in two starts since finishing second on debut; longer trip should suit

runners.name: Sogniamo
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in one start for Marcus Tregoning as a 2yo, but despite being sent of 80-1 she made a successful reappearance/stable debut in a Hamilton novice (1m1f, good to firm) last month; needs to back it up now switched to a handicap, but she remains completely unexposed.
runners.comment: 80-1 when winning at Hamilton last month; needs to back it up now in a handicap

runners.name: Northern Brave
runners.spotlight: Sent off 2-7 when slamming three rivals by upwards of 5l in an Ayr novice (1m, heavy) last October, but well held in two York handicaps (1m4f/1m2f) last month (behind Stoneacre Donny on both occasions); needs more.
runners.comment: Well held in two York handicaps last month, finishing behind Stoneacre Donny both times

runners.name: Knowledge
runners.spotlight: Narrowly made a winning debut in a Southwell novice (1m, AW) in January, but hasn't built on it in two starts over 1m2f on turf (gelded before latest handicap debut); needs a resurgence.
runners.comment: Won a Southwell novice in January, but hasn't built on it in two starts on turf

runners.name: Be Patient
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 1m on the Newcastle AW last autumn, improving from his debut to land a novice on the second occasion; the form has worked out quite well and the longer trip should suit on breeding; market useful on handicap/turf debut after 225 days off.
runners.comment: Won a 1m AW novice at Newcastle last autumn; watch market on handicap/turf debut

races.race_name: Come Racing On Ladies' Day Tomorrow Maiden Handicap
Twilight Racing Summer Series Maiden (Div I)

runners.name: Feel Happy
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly is a sister to 6f 2yo winner Jewelry, half-sister to two winners; dam 5f 2yo winner, half-sister to 1m4f Group 1 winner Bracelet; never far from the pace before fading into fourth behind the promising Sparan Nua (now unbeaten in three races) at Naas six weeks ago; should go close with normal improvement.
runners.comment: Should go close with normal improvement from debut behind the promising Sparan Nua at Naas
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Elusive Path
runners.spotlight: Brother to winners Elusive Heights and Erratic Path, half-brother to winners Unterberg (seven races) and Marmolata; dam a 7f Group 3 winner; looked a likely future winner when the trainer's second string on debut at Fairyhouse two weeks ago; stayed on well to take third, only 0.75l behind the yard's consistent 83-rated Krasimir.
runners.comment: Looked a likely future winner when the trainer's second string on debut at Fairyhouse

runners.name: Motta Alta
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when sixth off 13 on debut at Naas; has less than 3l to find with Feel Happy, but that rival should uphold the form.
runners.comment: Less than 3l to find with Feel Happy on Naas running but that rival should uphold the form

runners.name: Final Boss
runners.spotlight: Showed winning potential on the second of three runs at two, staying on well for third in a 6f maiden at the Curragh; failed to improve on that when fifth at Navan the following week; blinkered for the first time; makes seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Promising third over 6f at the Curragh on the second of two runs at two, blinkered now

runners.name: Blue Orquidea
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to two winners on the continent; dam 1m winner by High Chaparral; best watched.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two winners on the continent, dam 1m winner by High Chaparral, best watched

runners.name: Mrs Lady Bella
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; sister to two winners, half-sister to a winner also; dam from a speedy family.
runners.comment: Fifth foal, sister to two winners, half-sister to a winner also;, dam from a speedy family

runners.name: Tourin
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; Mehmas filly is closely related to winners Testa (French, Listed) and Malrescia (Group-placed at sprint trips), half-sister to three more, including Group 3-winning stayer Lismore; dam (by Whipper), a French winner; in good hands.
runners.comment: Newcomer by Mehmas, half-sister to Group 3-winning stayer Lismore, dam has a solid record

runners.name: Coolshine
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 7f 2yo winner First Prophet; unraced dam closely related to 1m Group 3 winner Rehn's Nest; decent pedigree; from a small Galway yard more typically associated with jumpers; may need to pick up experience.
runners.comment: Decent pedigree, may need to pick up experience, yard usually associated with jumpers

runners.name: Thumama Sands
runners.spotlight: Always at the back in the Fairyhouse race in which Elusive Path was third a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Always at the back in the Fairyhouse race in which Elusive Path was third a fortnight ago

runners.name: Mac An Luais
runners.spotlight: Gelded son of Gleneagles is the first foal out of an unraced Kingman half-sister to several winners; from the family 1m4f Group 1 winners Novellist and Magical Lagoon.
runners.comment: Gelding by Gleneagles out of an unraced Kingman mare, plenty of stamina in the family

runners.name: Banish Misfortune
runners.spotlight: First foal out of an unraced sister to Flat/hurdle winner Snookered; unlikely to feature on belated debut.
runners.comment: First foal out of an unraced sister to Flat/hurdle winner Snookered, unlikely to feature

runners.name: Quafftide
runners.spotlight: Not a bad run on debut at the Curragh in May last year; failed to build on that at Naas in October; shaped with some promise when fourth behind year-younger fillies in a 7f maiden at Leopardstown last week; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Ran only twice at three; shaped quite promisingly when fourth at Leopardstown last week

runners.name: Medusa King
runners.spotlight: Brother to 5f Listed winner winner Dickiedooda; no encouragement from debut in a 6f Cork maiden; tongue-tie and cheekpieces fitted now.
runners.comment: No encouragement from debut in a 6f Cork maiden, tongue-tie and cheekpieces fitted now

runners.name: Mountain Memory
runners.spotlight: Faded into fifth in testing ground in a 7f maiden at Cork a month ago; likely to improve, but others have stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Likely to improve from last month's debut at Cork, but others have stronger credentials

runners.name: Courtlough Gula
runners.spotlight: Last of 16 on only start at two; huge price and never a threat on her recent reappearance at Down Royal.
runners.comment: Last of 16 on only start at two; huge price and never a threat on her recent reappearance
runners.owner: Paul M Butler & James Patrick Byrne

runners.name: Shay's Dream
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at Dundalk on only start at two; towards rear at this venue on return; reserve.
runners.comment: Reserve, tailed off at Dundalk on only start at two, towards rear at this venue on return

runners.name: Omissions
runners.spotlight: A 60,000euros breeze-up purchase; ran only once at two for Natalia Lupini; some merit in his midfield run over 1m at the Curragh in October; has been gelded and should be more mature now; could make a useful recruit for Eoin Griffin.
runners.comment: Some merit in his midfield run at the Curragh in October, gelded and has changed stable

races.race_name: Twilight Racing Summer Series Maiden (Div II)

runners.name: Expert Dancer
runners.spotlight: Consistent in maidens, making the frame on all four occasions; disappointing on handicap debut over 1m at the Curragh in a visor (now discarded); faded in the final furlong, return to 7f may suit.
runners.comment: Unplaced in a 1m Curragh handicap last time, previous maiden form makes her a contender

runners.name: Quinta Girl
runners.spotlight: Best effort at two when a close fourth in a 6f Naas maiden; flopped in a Dundalk nursery on final start before the winter; now appeals as a possible future winner after Curragh third behind The Pipers Call, who had shown a useful standard of form at two.
runners.comment: Mixed form as a juvenile, now appeals as a possible future winner after Curragh third
runners.owner: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan

runners.name: Rebel Wave
runners.spotlight: New Bay filly's price rose from 110,000gns as a foal to 280,000euros as a yearling; some merit in her runs at Naas and Down Royal, but may need to get a handicap mark.
runners.comment: Some merit in her runs at Naas and Down Royal but may need to get a handicap mark

runners.name: Glasarmi
runners.spotlight: Two Gowran runs have been good enough to suggest she may pick up a race at some stage; others have stronger credentials, but she can be rated a possibility given just a little improvement.
runners.comment: Two Gowran runs have been good enough to suggest she may pick up a race at some stage
runners.owner: P R Dowling & Ms G Brennan

runners.name: Tokyo Treasure
runners.spotlight: Best of three runs last year when third over this trip at Down Royal; several younger rivals appeal more than this 75-rated gelding.
runners.comment: Best of three runs last year when third at Down Royal, several younger rivals appeal more

runners.name: Green Amoro
runners.spotlight: E140,000 yearling; first foal out of Que Amoro, a Listed sprint winner and runner-up to Battaash in 2020 Nunthorpe; second in a 7f barrier trial at Naas; improvement likely from fifth at Fairyhouse; worth monitoring how the third Elusive Path gets on in the first heat.
runners.comment: Improvement likely from debut fifth at Fairyhouse, third Elusive Path runs in first heat

runners.name: Winston's Promise
runners.spotlight: E32,000 foal purchase; gelded son of Churchill is the first foal out of a maiden by Kodiac; stable much better known for jumpers.
runners.comment: By Churchill, first foal out of a maiden by Kodiac; stable much better known for jumpers

runners.name: Strikepoint
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother by Phoenix Of Spain to the trainer's three-time winner Indigo Five; dam maiden, half-sister to 7f Group 3 winners Bounce The Blues and Jazz Princess.
runners.comment: Half-brother by Phoenix Of Spain to the trainer's useful three-time winner Indigo Five

runners.name: Sydney Dollars
runners.spotlight: Well supported for 1m debut at Naas last month but lost all chance with a slow start; no obvious excuse when soundly beaten in seventh at Leopardstown last week; reserve.
runners.comment: Reserve, lost all chance with a slow start at Naas; no obvious excuse at Leopardstown

runners.name: Dc Diva
runners.spotlight: Mild promise on only 2024 start for Kevin Coleman; missed last season; in rear on return at Cork last month.
runners.comment: Mild promise on only 2024 start, missed last season, in rear on return at Cork last month

runners.name: Banshof
runners.spotlight: 260,000gns yearling; unraced for the Crisfords; picked up cheaply last October; failed to make a significant impact on recent debut at Dundalk.
runners.comment: Unraced for the Crisfords, failed to make a significant impact on recent debut at Dundalk

runners.name: Hugo Spritz
runners.spotlight: Last of 14 first time out at Dundalk; struggled again on turf debut at Gowran.
runners.comment: Last of 14 first time out at Dundalk, struggled again on turf debut at Gowran

runners.name: Monmouth
runners.spotlight: Well-related Kingman colt; fair debut in a 6f Curragh maiden; something possibly amiss at Navan when he dropped away very tamely in the final quarter-mile; cheekpieces on now.
runners.comment: Fair debut in a 6f Curragh maiden, something possibly amiss at Navan, cheekpieces on now
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Pimlico Piranha
runners.spotlight: Big price on debut over 1m here last week; slowly away and never a threat.
runners.comment: Big price on debut over 1m here last week, slowly away and never a threat

runners.name: Hope N Pray
runners.spotlight: Nothing in her three starts to suggest any prospect of featuring in a maiden.
runners.comment: Nothing in her three starts to suggest any prospect of featuring in a maiden

runners.name: Poet's Willow
runners.spotlight: Three-figure odds for all five starts and has shown nothing; cheekpieces added now to tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Three-figure odds for all five starts, has shown nothing, cheekpieces added to tongue-tie

runners.name: Wheres The Gold
runners.spotlight: Failed to show anything in two starts at two for her previous trainer; safe to rule out after long absence.
runners.comment: Failed to show anything in two starts at two for her previous trainer, safe to rule out

runners.name: Chapel Lane
runners.spotlight: Placed in maidens at 6f and 1m last season; longer trip may not have been ideal on handicap/seasonal debut at Down Royal; first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Placed in maidens at 6f and 1m, longer trip may not have been ideal on handicap debut

runners.name: Watch Tower
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up at Dundalk late last year after showing ability in two runs on turf; can pick up a race like this if he has matured satisfactorily.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up at Dundalk late last year after showing ability in two runs on turf

runners.name: Substance
runners.spotlight: Has plenty to find with Zenford on Curragh running; the balance of his form is respectable, featuring a 4l defeat of Fortuity that earned him a 10lb rise; back in handicap company now after third of six in a Fairyhouse conditions race; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Has plenty to find with Zenford on Curragh running, the balance of his form is respectable

runners.name: Coincidental Glory
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Dundalk nursery last winter; has shown better form since finishing in rear in a Curragh race in which Zenford took second; that rival has the merit of consistency.
runners.comment: Has shown better form since in rear in a Curragh race in which Zenford took second

runners.name: Zenford
runners.spotlight: Placed at Leopardstown on only start at two; has developed a consistent level of form by making the first three in all three starts this term; went close at the Curragh in his first handicap; does not need to improve much to open his account now.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden, went close at the Curragh in his first handicap, holds a leading chance

runners.name: Halon Bay
runners.spotlight: Leopardstown winner last month on his third start; declared to run in a rated race at that venue on Thursday (6.20).
runners.comment: Leopardstown winner, also declared to run in a rated race at that venue on Thursday (6.20)

runners.name: Dawn Romance
runners.spotlight: 6f Naas maiden winner, down the field at the same venue on her handicap debut after starting the season with an ambitious bid at a Listed race at Cork; visored now.
runners.comment: 6f Naas maiden winner, down the field at the same venue on her handicap debut, visored now

runners.name: Love Orchid
runners.spotlight: Yet to reach the first three; appears well held by Fortuity on Roscommon running; needs to find considerable improvement in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Yet to reach the first three, appears well held by Fortuity on Roscommon running

runners.name: Venetian Star
runners.spotlight: Maiden has run several sound races in defeat for Johnny Murtagh and this yard; put a blot on his record at Fairyhouse last week; overall form, including a close third behind Fortuity on his penultimate start, gives him a place chance.
runners.comment: Put a blot on his record at Fairyhouse last week, overall form gives him a place chance

runners.name: Fortuity
runners.spotlight: Improved for the switch to handicaps and good ground when second at Roscommon last month before going one better at Fairyhouse; has got away with a 4lb rise; should remain competitive with Keithen Kennedy claiming 5lb.
runners.comment: Has got away with a 4lb rise for a Fairyhouse win; should remain competitive with 5lb off

runners.name: Thru And Thru
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced filly; second twice in maidens and made a bright start in handicaps when third at Naas (7f, soft) in March; something amiss when favourite at Leopardstown next time; weakened late on over 7f at that venue 15 days ago; the stiff mile may stretch her stamina.
runners.comment: Weakened late on over 7f at Leopardstown 15 days ago; the stiff mile may stretch her

runners.name: Tofino
runners.spotlight: Won a Curragh handicap (1m, good) last year; put in a satisfactory effort on return at Navan in April when tackling 1m2f for the first time and beaten only 3l into fifth over same trip at Leopardstown on latest; goes on any ground; worth considering.
runners.comment: Won a Curragh handicap (1m, good) last year; goes on any ground; worth considering

runners.name: Anvika
runners.spotlight: No win since June 2024 at this course (7f); third to Amemri in this race last year; standout effort last term a fine second in premier fillies' handicap at Leopardstown (1m, good to yielding); best run of the season at that venue eight days ago (1m1f, soft; Spanish Temptress fourth) when a staying-on third after a slow start; player.
runners.comment: Last win was here over 7f in 2024; chance judged on latest third at Leopardstown

runners.name: Una Matata
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; excellent record on AW of late, winning four of her last 10 starts; big eyecatcher at Down Royal (7f, good) three weeks ago when beaten 2l into fifth after a very slow start and not clear run 1f out but the forecast rain is a negative for her chances.
runners.comment: Good AW performer; eyecatcher at Down Royal last time; rain is a big negative
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Mark McGuckin & Claire Wylie

runners.name: Amemri
runners.spotlight: Three-time turf winner won this race last year (Anvika third); back to form when recording a 10th career win at Dundalk in March; runner-up over C&D (yielding; form has worked out well) on penultimate; troubled passage last time at Down Royal; wouldn't want it too soft.
runners.comment: Three-time turf winner won this race last year; had excuses last time; prefers good

runners.name: Spanish Temptress
runners.spotlight: Went up an aggregate 21lb for three handicap wins last season; unlucky in a couple of outings of late, beaten a nose over C&D (yielding) in April and just failed at Gowran (9.5f, yielding) on penultimate; solid fourth at Leopardstown (1m1f, soft; Anvika third) eight days ago and rider takes off valuable 5lb.
runners.comment: Progressive last season; unlucky a couple of times this term; good claimer; in the mix

runners.name: Gloriously Glam
runners.spotlight: Was gaining a fourth career victory when landing 7f Gowran claimer (good to yielding) last month; beaten favourite next time in Fairyhouse claimer; finished last of eight in 7f Gowran handicap last week but she's 3lb below her last winning mark, will appreciate the forecast rain and could bounce back under 7lb claimer.
runners.comment: Finished last of eight in 7f Gowran handicap last week; 3lb below last winning mark
runners.owner: Dooley Thoroughbreds & J Fairhurst & Deepak Dhand

runners.name: Fratas
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose prominent racer who contested the 2024 Triumph Hurdle; regressive form on the Flat last summer and was last seen tailed off over hurdles at the Galway festival; subsequently sold for 7,000euros; market can guide on first run for top yard after a layoff.
runners.comment: Regressive form last summer; sold cheaply; market can guide on first run for O'Brien

runners.name: Bella Colombia
runners.spotlight: Added a second AW win at Dundalk in March (1m); 0-14 on turf but has run some decent races in defeat; last two runs (over C&D and at Rocommon) have been satisfactory.
runners.comment: Two AW wins, 0-14 turf record is a concern though her last two runs have been satisfactory

runners.name: Heart Of Darkness
runners.spotlight: AW winner is 0-11 on turf; twice well beaten on grass since Dundalk fifth behind Bella Colombia.
runners.comment: AW winner 0-11 on turf, well beaten twice since Dundalk fifth behind Bella Colombia
runners.owner: B O'Sullivan/D Whyte/Exors of the Late D McHugh/M O'Loghlen

runners.name: Bright Image
runners.spotlight: Encouraging effort when fifth at the Curragh in March 2024; off for a long time after that; has belatedly shown signs of winning potential with fourth placings here and at Gowran; can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Has belatedly shown signs of winning potential with fourth placings here and at Gowran

runners.name: Carvalhal
runners.spotlight: Winner over 1m1f on quick ground in Britain; placed three times in a row last June when headgear went on; form tailed off in the autumn; strong chance now based on last week's second over 7f at this venue.
runners.comment: Fair form at around this time last year, strong chance now based on last week's second

runners.name: Limbocall
runners.spotlight: Placed in a maiden last year but well beaten; appeared not to stay 1m1f on handicap debut at Leopardstown last August; absent since.
runners.comment: Placed in a maiden last year but well beaten, appeared not to stay 1m1f on handicap debut

runners.name: Liamo
runners.spotlight: Sixth at Listowel when failing to repeat a 2025 win that represents his only win from 21 starts; consistent if limited last season; place chance if reproducing best form.
runners.comment: Sixth at Listowel when failing to repeat a 2025 win, consistent if limited last season

runners.name: Friars Gate
runners.spotlight: Ran her best race so far when fourth at 40-1 here last week, a head in front of fifth-placed Notforalongtime; may be on the upgrade; second reserve
runners.comment: Second reserve, ran her best race so far when fourth here last week, Notforalongtime fifth

runners.name: Saith Seren
runners.spotlight: Most recent run was over hurdles; before that, a fair fifth in a 1m Killarney handicap, a run good enough to bring her into the equation here; most of her best form has been at Killarney.
runners.comment: Most recent run was over hurdles, before that a fair fifth in a 1m Killarney handicap

runners.name: Racing Royalty
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner on soft ground in 2023; placed at Galway last September before two below-par runs; seventh of 14 here last week on seasonal debut; held by Carvalhal; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, seventh of 14 here last week on seasonal debut, held by Carvalhal

runners.name: Oitentinha
runners.spotlight: Not a bad run at Fairyhouse a fortnight ago on stable debut after a long absence; improvement is feasible.
runners.comment: Not a bad run at Fairyhouse on stable debut after a long absence, improvement is feasible

runners.name: Pure Valour
runners.spotlight: Weak maiden form; chance is not obvious, perhaps tongue-tie will spark improvement, but others have much stronger claims.
runners.comment: Weak maiden form, chance is not obvious, perhaps tongue-tie will spark improvement
runners.owner: Mrs Annette Coogan & J M O'Reilly & Frank Condra

runners.name: Crosshaven
runners.spotlight: Dropped away from two furlongs out on debut over 1m2f at the Curragh last May; not seen again after a moderate run over 1m5f at Leopardstown in June; hard to fancy after a moderate 1m run at Killarney.
runners.comment: Ran over longer distances last season, hard to fancy after a moderate 1m run at Killarney

runners.name: Captain Ciano
runners.spotlight: Best run was at an early stage of last season; a long way below that standard this year, apart from a Dundalk fifth during the winter.
runners.comment: Best run was at an early stage of last season, a long way below that standard this year

runners.name: Starting Monday
runners.spotlight: Ended a long losing sequence with Gowran win in April, the third success of a 44-race career that has also seen him place on eight occasions; not beaten far in fifth at Cork on latest.
runners.comment: Ended a long losing sequence with Gowran win in April, not beaten far in fifth on latest

runners.name: Notforalongtime
runners.spotlight: Prolific AW winner (9-32); off-putting 1-30 record on turf but some good placings this season at Cork and the Curragh; finished only a head behind fourth-placed Friars Gate here last week; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, prolific AW winner, off-putting 1-30 on turf, boasts useful form this term

runners.name: Peig Sayers
runners.spotlight: Has struggled in her three handicap attempts, hard to fancy despite a reduced mark, down 9lb form initial rating.
runners.comment: Has struggled in her three handicap attempts, hard to fancy despite a reduced mark

runners.name: Green Icon
runners.spotlight: Both wins came over 7f for Owen Burrows early in his career; yet to find his form for this yard; gave a mildly encouraging display in finishing in midfield when a 66-1 chance at the Curragh.
runners.comment: Two wins over 7f for Owen Burrows early in his career, yet to find his form for this yard

runners.name: Monroe Dasher
runners.spotlight: Clipped heels and slipped up on only handicap start last season; has looked very limited, but still early days; worth a look for future reference.
runners.comment: Clipped heels and slipped up on only handicap start last season, still early days

runners.name: Lady Crossing
runners.spotlight: Never closer than eighth place in nine outings; last of 14 at Dundalk on handicap debut; transformation needed.
runners.comment: Never closer than eighth place in nine outings, transformation needed

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Martin Molony Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Goodie Two Shoes
runners.spotlight: Third runner for yard; dual-purpose performer, 6-12 on Flat, 1-7 over jumps; highly progressive last year, winning 1m6f and 2m Listed at the Curragh and Down Royal before Group 3 victory at Fairyhouse and third in the Group 2 Lillie Langtry at Goodwood in August; rounded off the year with an excellent runner-up in the Melbourne Cup; fitness has to be taken on trust but big player if she's ready.
runners.comment: Two Listed and Group 3 wins last year and placed in Melbourne Cup; big player on return

runners.name: Happy Pharoah
runners.spotlight: Strong form at Leopardstown last season, winning three times, including a Listed contest and premier handicap (both over 1m5f on good to yielding); never a factor in the Irish Cesarewitch; unsuited by very soft ground on return at the Curragh before excellent runner-up to Scandinavia in the Saval Beg at Leopardstown (1m6f, good); leading form chance but soft ground would be a concern.
runners.comment: Repeat of second to Scandinavia may be enough but soft ground would be a concern

runners.name: Arouet
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced last year at three, winning minor Roscommon contest and chased home a very smart type in Leopardstown Listed over this trip; recently landed the odds in Cork 4yo maiden hurdle before beaten around 8l into third in 1m4f Curragh Listed last month; has a bit to find.
runners.comment: Recent win over hurdles before moderate third in Curragh Listed over this trip; needs more

runners.name: New Zealand
runners.spotlight: Won Curragh 2yo maiden last year (7f, good) and twice campaigned in Group company, notably fifth in Group 1 Criterium International; underwhelming seasonal debut in Epsom Classic Trial and again weakened quite tamely at Navan (1m5f) in first-time blinkers on latest; cheekpieces are reached for now.
runners.comment: Won 7f Curragh maiden and solid Group 1 run at two; nowhere near that level this term
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier/Michael Tabor/Derrick Smith/Peter M Brant

runners.name: Leinster
runners.spotlight: Fourth in Chester Cup and Ascot Stakes last season; won four-runner St Leger Trial at the Curragh in August; failed to do himself justice when tailed off in Irish St Leger; made late progress into fourth behind Scandinavia at Navan on return; ran to a similar level when fourth of six in Group 3 Saval Beg at Leopardstown; should be sharper now.
runners.comment: Fourth in Chester Cup and Ascot Stakes last season; two fair efforts so far this term

runners.name: That's Amore
runners.spotlight: Powered 6l clear on debut at Newbury (1m, heavy) in October 2024 and was instantly talked about as an Oaks contender for the following season; that failed to go to plan when fourth to Minnie Hauk in Cheshire Oaks; not seen again for 10 months when winning 1m3f Southwell novice (AW); disappointed at Nottingham last time when keen to post, ran with plenty of zest and weakened; too soon to write her off and likely has a better chance than bare ratings suggest.
runners.comment: Hasn't gone on from impressive debut win but too early to write her off; live outsider

runners.name: Bubble Gum
runners.spotlight: Finished midfield in French and German Oaks in 2024; Listed winner at Cork (1m4f, good) for Paddy Twomey on Irish debut; failed to build on that; three moderate runs this season but not beaten far in 1m1f Navan Listed 12 days ago in first-time blinkers; they are replaced with cheekpieces now.
runners.comment: Listed win for Paddy Twomey but hasn't done a lot since, for all ran okay at Navan lto

runners.name: Cristal Clere
runners.spotlight: Third career win came on final start last season in 1m Listowel Listed in testing conditions under a positive ride (just a day after being well beaten in a handicap); failed to beat a single rival home in 1m4f Curragh Listed on return; balance of form is over shorter than this so others preferred.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival home in 1m4f Curragh Listed on return; best form is over shorter

runners.name: Uxmal
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; hasn't had much racing for his age; won the Queen Alexandra Stakes at Royal Ascot in 2024 and beaten 7l by Al Riffa in last year's Curragh Cup; finished 26l behind Chally Chute when remote fifth in Group 3 Loughbrown Stakes in November; returns from a break with plenty to prove now.
runners.comment: Finished 26l behind Chally Chute when last seen so has plenty to prove on his return

runners.name: Chally Chute
runners.spotlight: In the form of his life last year, winning 1m4f Roscommon Listed on seasonal debut at 125-1 and better again when upped to 2m at the Curragh, unlucky narrow defeat in Irish Cesarewitch and ready winner of Group 3 Loughbrown Stakes in November on heavy; will appreciate the forecast rain and has to be respected on return.
runners.comment: In the form of his life last year culminating in Group 3 win; loves testing ground; player

runners.name: Layfayette
runners.spotlight: Ten-time winner, including this race in 2023 (third in this contest last year); won good-class Curragh conditions event (1m6f, soft to heavy) on return; never able to land a blow in the Vintage Crop on quicker ground; first attempt at 2m on latest when runner-up to Carmers in Down Royal Listed (good); each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2023 and third in it last year; won on return and good 2m effort latest

runners.name: Sunchart
runners.spotlight: Useful dual-purpose sort, 3-35 on Flat and second on 10 occasions; his only two stakes race wins from 29 attempts have been in the 1m2f Naas event in which he was third of eight on return; well beaten in the Vintage Crop on latest but will be more suited by testing conditions.
runners.comment: Well beaten in the Vintage Crop on latest but will be more suited by testing conditions

runners.name: Taj Crown
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and ran green at Dundalk on her only start at three; made late progress in 1m maiden at Killarney on return and again came late and fast when runner-up in 1m4f course claimer (good to yielding) nine days ago; leading contender.
runners.comment: Came late and fast when runner-up in 1m4f course claimer nine days ago; leading contender

runners.name: Blissful Bonita
runners.spotlight: Didn't count but wasn't beaten far on last month's handicap debut at Killarney (1m3f, good); ran on well in the closing stages when fifth of 16 in course handicap (1m4f, good to yielding) nine days ago in cheekpieces (retained), in front of Cascade Canyon and Royal Sapphire; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Ran on well in closing stages when fifth of 16 here (1m4f, good to yielding) nine days ago

runners.name: Wrob And Wryan
runners.spotlight: Some good efforts on the AW last year, including a staying-on fifth in 10.5f handicap in September off 69; never involved at Gowran last week on return which accumulates to four poor turf efforts.
runners.comment: Never involved at Gowran last week on return which accumulates to four poor turf efforts

runners.name: Mare Crisium
runners.spotlight: Made some late headway on debut at the Curragh when finishing sixth (10.5f, soft); failed to progress from that when well beaten at Cork (1m4f, soft to heavy) last month but she's still considered in a weak maiden.
runners.comment: Handles soft judged on her debut run and that effort brings her into the equation

runners.name: Themis
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; four runs have amounted to little and again ran poorly on handicap debut at Leopardstown 15 days ago; Irish Oaks entry is unrealistic but evidently held in some regard so could make an impact taking a sizeable step up in trip.
runners.comment: Poor form but holds Irish Oaks entry and this is her first crack at a trip this far

runners.name: Cascade Canyon
runners.spotlight: Best of three maiden runs was on debut when sixth of 13 at Navan (1m2f, heavy); well beaten here nine days ago on 1m4f handicap debut but faces an easier assignment here and may improve if the ground deteriorates.
runners.comment: Faces an easier assignment here and may improve if the ground deteriorates

runners.name: Second Line
runners.spotlight: Hasn't troubled the judge in three runs; beaten 10l on latest at Killarney (1m) but it's a race that has worked out very well with the second, third and ninth all winning since; needs to improve plenty but may do so.
runners.comment: Yet to trouble the judge but the form of his last Killarney run has worked out very well

runners.name: An Cailleach
runners.spotlight: Completely tailed off at Gowran and Leopardstown; can be ignored; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Completely tailed off at Gowran and Leopardstown; can be ignored; cheekpieces

runners.name: Tintinabulla
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure odds and never involved at Navan and Leopardstown; unlikely to feature.
runners.comment: Triple-figure odds and never involved at Navan and Leopardstown; unlikely to feature

runners.name: Ravens Can Fly
runners.spotlight: Never dangerous on his Gowran debut (1m, good to yielding) and similar effort when upped to 1m4f at Fairyhouse last week; others preferred.
runners.comment: Never dangerous on his Gowran debut and similar effort when upped to 1m4f at Fairyhouse

runners.name: It's Good To Talk
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a heavy-ground bumper at Naas on debut in February; well beaten in a bumper and two Flat outings since; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Well beaten in a bumper and two Flat outings since debut bumper second; others appeal more

runners.name: Royal Sapphire
runners.spotlight: Sent off at large prices on all eight outings to date and yet to show a hint of ability; hails from top yard but first-time blinkers will need to have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Poor form; hails from top yard but first-time blinkers will need to have a positive effect

runners.name: Faithful Follower
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler hasn't looked like adding to his tally in four starts on the Flat; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler hasn't looked like adding to his tally in four starts on the Flat

runners.name: Battle Of Maldon
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in first three maidens (7f-1m2f) on a variety of ground, the form of his Navan run working out well; made the trip to Chepstow worthwhile when getting up late on to win (1m2f, good to soft); the one to beat on handicap debut in receipt of the 3yo allowance.
runners.comment: Good maiden form & got off the mark at Chepstow last time; the one to beat on handicap bow

runners.name: Quatre Bras
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner (twice over 10.5f at Dundalk); out of form on turf last season but some good efforts this term, second at Naas (7f, soft) in March and dead-heating for fourth at Leopardstown (1m2f) when not clear run; down the field last two starts; stays this far.
runners.comment: Three-time AW winner is yet to win on turf but has run well this term; stays this far

runners.name: Genoah
runners.spotlight: Four wins on the AW at 10.5f-1m4f; plenty to prove on this surface and soft would be a major concern; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Plenty to prove on this surface and soft would be a major concern; new headgear

runners.name: Slurricane
runners.spotlight: Useful hurdler who has won twice on the Flat, including off 3lb lower at Galway in October (1m4f, heavy); ran respectably at Gowran (1m6f, soft) in April but below his best on good at Fairyhouse last week; each-way player if the rain comes.
runners.comment: Below his best on good at Fairyhouse last week; each-way player if the rain comes

runners.name: Dawn Coming
runners.spotlight: Modest form on Flat for Jim Bolger; made the frame on all five hurdle starts for this yard; makes Flat handicap debut off a potentially lenient mark; shortlisted and of definite interest if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: Solid hurdles form; shortlisted and of definite interest if strong in the betting
runners.owner: Ms P Twiss & J Twiss & M O'Sullivan & S Nevin Twiss

runners.name: Eben Zaabeel
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden in Britain for Charlie Hills, runner-up in AW handicaps final two starts in 2024; yet to match that form in Ireland; only if interest if the market speaks in his favour.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden in Britain for Charlie Hills; yet to match that form in Ireland

runners.name: Mighty Wave
runners.spotlight: Good run on second start at Gowran (1m, soft) when staying on into third, beaten 2.75l; below that level on better ground at Leopardstown (1m4f) but that was a hot contest; blinkered for handicap debut; interesting despite 2lb wrong at weights.
runners.comment: Some eyecatching maiden form; blinkered for handicap debut; interesting despite 2lb wrong

runners.name: Satono Chevalier
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner is 2-15 on turf; has notched up back-to-back wins at Navan (1m2f, heavy) and on debut for this yard at Ballinrobe (1m5f, good to yielding); well beaten at Gowran 18 days ago; handicapper looks in control for now.
runners.comment: Back-to-back wins but down the field at Gowran so handicapper looks in control now

runners.name: Dark Summit
runners.spotlight: French winner (1m, good to soft); shaped promisingly enough on debut for this yard in the Irish Lincoln; confidence dented by three more recent runs; others preferred.
runners.comment: Shaped promisingly enough on debut for this yard in the Irish Lincoln but poor since

runners.name: Palace Rock
runners.spotlight: Rated 125 over hurdles; sole Flat win on AW in 2021; winless since hurdles win in 2022; not seen since pulled up over fences at Naas in January 2025; best watched.
runners.comment: Rated 125 over hurdles; sole Flat win on AW in 2021; off the track since January 2025
runners.owner: A G Beatty & P McBride & Mrs Peter Casey & Mrs Paula Casey

runners.name: Chemdawg
runners.spotlight: Sole Flat win in Roscommon maiden (1m4f, soft) in May 2024; 1-19 over hurdles, that win gained when making all to score decisively at Kilbeggan in April; second in course handicap hurdle on latest but tough mark back on Flat.
runners.comment: In good form over hurdles but runs off a tough mark back on the Flat

races.race_name: Zyn In-The-Moment Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Scommessa Sicura
runners.spotlight: Clear second to Silent Beauty (due to run in the Albany at Ascot today), despite edging both ways, on debut and built on that at Newbury last time; has the edge in experience and sets the standard the newcomers are gunning for.
runners.comment: Has the edge in experience and sets the standard the newcomers are gunning for

runners.name: Musical Times
runners.spotlight: Second foal of Cheshire Oaks (Listed) winner Dubai Fountain, who won on her 2yo debut (7f) for the Johnston yard; trainer just getting going with 2yos for the season (unsurprisingly has excellent record with them here down the years); interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Second foal of Cheshire Oaks (Listed) winner Dubai Fountain; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Lady Miranda
runners.spotlight: Early-foaled 65,000gns foal, 85,000euros yearling whose dam is well related but proved nothing exciting at about 1m (produced a German 9.5f winner); holds a couple of sales race entries in the autumn; trainer enjoyed a fair first half to the year with 2yos.
runners.comment: 65,000gns foal, 85,000euros yearling; holds a couple of sales race entries in the autumn

runners.name: Celestia
runners.spotlight: Daughter of the yard's 1m2f Listed winner Natavia, who's produced a St Leger runner-up in Haskoy (demoted to fourth); trainer yet to get rolling for the year with juveniles (three runners all well held at big prices); the market will guide.
runners.comment: Daughter of yard's Listed winner Natavia; trainer yet to get rolling for year with 2yos

runners.name: Princess Lottie
runners.spotlight: By Camlelot out of a mare who's produced a 5f AW winner; trainer had none placed from six 2yo runners this season and doesn't have a great record with them here all told; looks to be flying high enough on debut.
runners.comment: Trainer had none placed from six 2yo runners this season; looks to be flying high enough
runners.owner: The Ten Fools & A Horse Partnership

runners.name: Carpe Portus
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to French 7f 2yo winner Syros and a winner in Qatar; trainer boasts an ordinary record with 2yos on both Newmarket courses (2-47 here) but she's a likely type on paper under Jack Callan, who's enjoyed a good spell of late.
runners.comment: Half-sister to French 7f 2yo winner Syros and a winner in Qatar; a likely type on paper

runners.name: Magic Show
runners.spotlight: Trainer won this last year with this filly's half-sister Date Line (favourite; second time out); also a half-sister to five other winners, including the yard's Carolina Reaper (C&D); the market should shed some light.
runners.comment: Half-sister to the winner of this race last year for same yard; one of the likelier types

runners.name: Divine Concerto
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to yard's Baker Blue, who won two sprints last winter (improved with racing; seems better suited by the AW than turf); trainer ended a losing run earlier in the week but had a quiet year with 2yos and is 0-21 with them at the Newmarket courses all told.
runners.comment: Half-sister to yard's Baker Blue (won two AW sprints); trainer quiet thus far with 2yos

runners.name: Beautiful Effort
runners.spotlight: Daughter of two winners for her connections (sire 5f-7f, dam 8.6f); started a single-figure price for her Goodwood debut four weeks but blew the start, pulled hard and finished a remote last; evidently felt capable of better but needs to settle up from 6f.
runners.comment: Pulled hard when a remote last on debut; evidently felt capable of more but has to settle

races.race_name: Zyn Pacesetter British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Hatteen
runners.spotlight: 0-4 but he's recorded an RPR of 81 when runner-up in a Nottingham novice and Newbury maiden (both at 1m2f on good) this spring; major player on that form and he still has potential for further progress.
runners.comment: RPR of 81 when runner-up in two 1m2f events (good) this spring; key player

runners.name: Superior Choice
runners.spotlight: 1,100,000gns yearling; Dubawi filly out of 1m6f Group 1 winner; made a promising start when fourth at Newmarket last October and he built on that when scoring in good style on his reappearance at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) in April; has a penalty but he's open to more progress and the Gosden yard has won this in four times since 2019.
runners.comment: Won on Leicester reappearance and his yard has won this four times since 2019; respected

runners.name: Act Of Generosity
runners.spotlight: 875,000gns yearling; 2-1 shot on Leicester debut in April but he looked inexperienced and finished an 8l fifth of six behind Superior Choice; should be more streetwise this time but he needs plenty of improvement.
runners.comment: 875,000gns yearling; 2-1 at Leicester but he was well held behind Superior Choice

runners.name: Ragazzo Grandi
runners.spotlight: 58,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to 5f 2yo winner Fox Degree (RPR 77); dam maiden (56) half-sister to 10.5f Listed winner Lady Penko and useful 11.6f/1m4f winner Lostwithiel, out of half-sister to 1m4f Group/Grade 1 winner Erupt; this looks a tough starting point and he's probably best watched.
runners.comment: 58,000gns yearling; this looks a tough starting point and he's probably best watched

runners.name: Zurna
runners.spotlight: Massive prices and he's been well held in two novice events (1m2f, good to soft) this season; showed some ability at Windsor last time but handicaps look the way forward after this.
runners.comment: Massive prices and he's been well held in two novice events (1m2f) this season

runners.name: Eazy On The Eye
runners.spotlight: Wins come on Tapeta and has lacked consistency on grass; withdrawn at Lingfield (turf) late last month having got upset at the stalls (100-30 at the time) and is on a fair mark if in a better frame of mind under experienced 7lb claimer Harry Vigors.
runners.comment: Got upset at the stalls on her last intended run; fair mark if in a better frame of mind

runners.name: Stenmark
runners.spotlight: Became his new trainer's first runner, having been picked up from Denis Quinn for 17,000gns, when faring well in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Yarmouth three weeks ago (Dapper Guest behind); generally consistent and won't mind the stiffer 7f.
runners.comment: Solid debut for his new yard last time; generally consistent and won't mind the stiffer 7f

runners.name: Miletus
runners.spotlight: Expensive to follow since picked up from Charles Hills (turned over three times as favourite this year) and is tried in first-time cheekpieces (wore a hood at Yarmouth in the spring); a good test over a stiff 7f is fine but he doesn't always convince.
runners.comment: A good test over a stiff 7f is fine but he doesn't always convince; tried in cheekpieces

runners.name: Gallant
runners.spotlight: Picked up from Andrew Balding for 60,000gns not long after an AW win (1m) this time last year; not the easiest (can lug left) and will need to settle better after a spell sprinting, over tonight's stiff 7f, but he's capable off this mark if things fall right.
runners.comment: Will need to settle better upped from 6f but is capable off this mark if things fall right

runners.name: Dashing Dick
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner who started the season high in the weights, having closed out last year with a victory on the Rowley Mile, and has struggled; does most racing these days over 6f and needs a revival.
runners.comment: Dual course winner who started the season high in the weights and has struggled

runners.name: Madame De Sevigne
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Robert Eddery came over a stiff, undulating 7f, so has that in her favour; lacked consistency for new yard, below par in first-time cheekpieces latest (headgear now off, tongue-tie on), but could easily bounce back.
runners.comment: Two wins for Robert Eddery came over a stiff, undulating 7f; could easily bounce back

runners.name: Potomac River
runners.spotlight: Poor handicap debut last month, having been gelded and undergone wind surgery, when beaten a long way at Yarmouth; maybe that was down to the rain-softened ground but it leaves him something to prove; would want to see some market support.
runners.comment: Poor handicap debut leaves him something to prove; would want to see some market support

runners.name: Bravo Zulu
runners.spotlight: Said to have run flat last time; tended to respond well to switches/removal of headgear in the past (back to cheekpieces from blinkers tonight) and won't mind coming back up from sprint trips, but Pontefract will need forgiving.
runners.comment: Won't mind coming back up from sprint trips, but Pontefract will need forgiving

runners.name: Cherry Cobbler
runners.spotlight: Won an ordinary apprentice handicap easily under 8lb claimer Chloe Lyons on the Rowley Mile last month and was handed a stiff 8lb rise for it; ran to a similar level off his revised mark in better company (soft) last time to confirm himself still in form.
runners.comment: Stiff 8lb rise for his win last month; ran to a similar level off his revised mark latest

runners.name: Dapper Guest
runners.spotlight: Yard having a quiet year thus far and this gelding is a deal higher (12lb) in the weights than when winning this in first-time cheekpieces 12 months ago; not in much form this season and has to prove this mark is within range on turf.
runners.comment: Deal higher (12lb) in the weights than when winning this 12 months ago; not in much form

runners.name: Physique
runners.spotlight: Only ever won twice and been dry in almost two years; back in form last time, from the front (given no peace) without the usual hood and that gave something to build on off the same mark; goes on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Back in form last time, from the front (given no peace); goes on the shortlist

runners.name: Noble Guest
runners.spotlight: Dual Yarmouth winner over this trip last year, latterly off tonight's mark; will need a career-best effort if he's to take this and his last couple of runs suggest that may not be forthcoming.
runners.comment: Will need a career-best effort and last couple of runs suggest that may not be forthcoming

runners.name: Great Mates
runners.spotlight: Inclined to pull hard in two runs in handicaps early in the year for Michael Wigham and no surprise to see a hood on for her new yard as she returns to turf and drops from 1m; this mark should be within range if more amenable back from four months off.
runners.comment: No surprise to see hood on for new yard as she returns to turf and drops from 1m; possible

runners.name: Best Rate
runners.spotlight: Just a 2yo novice win to his name and expensive to follow but he's generally run consistently in some competitive handicaps over the past year or so; another solid effort back on turf last week, minus the blinkers, and looks set to go well once more.
runners.comment: Generally run consistently in some competitive handicaps over the past year or so; player

runners.name: Drifts Away
runners.spotlight: Big improver in the first half of last year, with four wins; struggled in his final two runs but remains feasibly treated on his best work and is still lightly raced on turf; market useful back from ten months off.
runners.comment: Big improver in the first half of last year; market useful back from ten months off

runners.name: Longlai
runners.spotlight: Won first time out twice in the past, which is a positive back from seven months off, although he's getting on now; a long time since he was seen regularly on turf and doesn't run at shy of 1m often, meaning others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Won first time out twice in the past; doesn't run at shy of 1m often now though; bit on

runners.name: Benacre
runners.spotlight: Not won on turf since his 2yo days and has gone off the boil, finishing a remote last behind Physique at Beverley a few weeks ago; hard to recommend off the back of that.
runners.comment: Gone off the boil, finishing a remote last behind Physique at Beverley a few weeks ago

runners.name: Fifty Nifty
runners.spotlight: Useful 3yo for Tom Clover, including a C&D win, who proved hard to place last year; handicapper given him a chance now, but he ran below market expectations for another new yard back from wind surgery last time; risks involved in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Below market billing for another new yard back from wind surgery latest; risks involved

runners.name: Al Karrar
runners.spotlight: Missed a huge chunk of his career, having finhished second in a Group 2 as a 2yo, and was below market expectations back from another layoff a month ago; the return to turf should suit, though, down another 3lb, and it's too soon to write him off.
runners.comment: The return to turf should suit down another 3lb, and it's too soon to write him off

runners.name: Outflank
runners.spotlight: Fair form when third over 1m3f, 1m4f and 1m2f on all three starts, ridden by Luke Catton on last two occasions; needs extra on this handicap debut and seemingly not the most straightforward, but blinkers go on and the feeling persists that he is capable of better.
runners.comment: Probably capable of better; seemingly not the most straightforward, but blinkers go on

runners.name: Hard To Believe
runners.spotlight: Won at Bath (1m, good to soft) last October on second start and followed up in Wolverhampton novice (9.4f, AW) in March; only seventh of 14 when 2-1 favourite on handicap debut at York (1m4f, good) five weeks ago but he should retain potential.
runners.comment: 7th of 14 when favourite on York handicap debut (1m4f, good) but should retain potential
runners.owner: Jayar Investments & Mrs L E Ramsden

runners.name: Battosai
runners.spotlight: Improved form with blinkers added (they've been kept since) when second of 12 in handicap at Salisbury (1m2f, good) in May; fair front-running effort 13 days later; dam 1m4f winner and it's possible he will find extra over this new trip, but it is needed.
runners.comment: Dam 1m4f winner and it's possible he will find extra over this new trip, but it is needed

runners.name: Brave Hunter
runners.spotlight: 0-5, clear best effort when second of 12 on handicap debut at Windsor (1m, good to firm) on penultimate outing; brother to a fairly useful 1m4f winner for the same connections, so today's extra 4f is interesting.
runners.comment: Brother to a fairly useful 1m4f winner for same connections; the extra 4f is interesting

runners.name: Outback Legend
runners.spotlight: Third of five at Chester (10.3f, good) on his second start and looked well suited by the extra distance when he gradually worked his way to the front in nine-runner maiden at Haydock (11.6f, soft) four weeks ago; more is needed on this handicap debut but he's going the right way.
runners.comment: Gradually worked his way to the front in nine-runner maiden at Haydock (11.6f, soft)

runners.name: Temple
runners.spotlight: Third at Nottingham (1m, soft) on second start last October and not discredited when 25-1 in a novice won under Sorin Moldoveanu by a much shorter-priced stablemate at Southwell (1m4f, AW) on April reappearance; 575,000gns yearling whose dam was useful over 1m4f+, so all in all he needs a close look on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Dam was useful over 1m4f+; all in all he needs a close look on handicap debut

runners.name: Fort Rock
runners.spotlight: Won novice contests at Kempton (7f, AW) last autumn on first two starts; reappeared with encouraging third on handicap debut on the other Newmarket course (1m, good to firm) last month and this very lightly raced 3yo could have lots more to offer for his top yard; wears first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 2-2 prior to encouraging handicap debut on the Rowley Mile; could have lots more to offer

runners.name: Stem
runners.spotlight: Runner-up last September in his first two handicaps (1m2f/1m, soft/good to soft) and not beaten far when ninth of 24 on reappearance in the Spring Cup at Newbury (1m, good); perhaps unsuited by Epsom when down the field a fortnight ago and this unexposed 4yo remains one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Perhaps unsuited by Epsom last time and this unexposed 4yo remains one to be interested in

runners.name: Spanish Voice
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten at Newbury (1m, good) last time following a 9lb rise for his win on the other Newmarket course earlier last month (1m, good to firm; made virtually all); however, this lightly raced 4yo impressed on that penultimate outing and his current mark may not be beyond him.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten at Newbury but impressive on Rowley Mile previously; still lightly raced

runners.name: Elan d'Or
runners.spotlight: Built upon previous promise (including on turf) to win two nurseries in a row at the end of last year (7f/1m, AW); respectable fifth on last month's reappearance on the other Newmarket course (1m, good to firm) and could show the benefit of that outing today; possible player.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with two wins and last month's reappearance 5th gives him something to build on

runners.name: Magnatura
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-5 in last year's 2yo season when winning at Goodwood (7f, good) last October on second nursery start; promising fourth on last month's reappearance at Newbury (1m, good) and he could have a part to play.
runners.comment: 2-5 in 2yo campaign; promising fourth on reappearance and could have a part to play

runners.name: Synners Kid
runners.spotlight: Won 1m nurseries last autumn on final two runs for Joseph O'Brien (yielding to soft/soft); he finished sixth last month on his two runs for Lemos De Souza (7f/1m, good/good to firm) and needs something extra on his first start for Australian trainer Henry Dwyer.
runners.comment: 2-2 in nurseries last autumn; sixth on both runs this year; needs something extra

runners.name: Arctic Thunder
runners.spotlight: 0-14 since winning on handicap debut in May 2024 and not at the top of his game on his three starts this season; however, he took a step back in the right direction when sixth of 16 over 7f at Epsom a fortnight ago, when making late gains, and this belated return to 1m is worth a go; chance.
runners.comment: Made late gains over 7f at Epsom a fortnight ago; this belated return to 1m is worth a go

runners.name: Angel Hunter
runners.spotlight: Two-time turf winner who scored at Lingfield (1m, AW) in April and is just 1lb higher here; however, he's finished unplaced on turf on his last three starts and needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: AW winner in April but unplaced on turf on his three starts since

runners.name: Release The Storm
runners.spotlight: Won novice races last summer on first two runs (7f, good/good to firm); well beaten at 50-1 in Haydock Listed race (7f, good) last month on his return, but could be a player if tapping back into last year's promise.
runners.comment: Won last summer on first 2 starts; well beaten in Listed race on return; retains potential

runners.name: Dark Tornado
runners.spotlight: Made it two wins in a row when scoring off this mark on handicap debut on the other Newmarket course (7f, good to firm) last May; however, has to produce more than he's managed on his two runs this term; visor goes on.
runners.comment: Won off this mark last May but has to produce more than he's managed this season

runners.name: Loz Vegas
