runners.spotlight: Glimpses of significant promise in light 2yo campaign last year and beaten only about 4l when sixth of ten on last month's handicap/seasonal debut at Newbury (1m, good); open to improvement and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Glimpses of significant promise in 2yo campaign; open to improvement on second run of year

runners.name: Spell Master
runners.spotlight: Won last October (7f, AW) on final run for Andrew Balding and he's also won on fast turf; couldn't get into it on recent stable/seasonal debut at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) but may have needed the run and his trainer had an impressive winner last week with one having his second run for the yard.
runners.comment: No impact on stable debut but could be different proposition with that run under his belt

runners.name: Mythical Guest
runners.spotlight: Second in this two years ago and often ran well last season; on a competitive mark on his best form but beaten on his last 20 starts and he's struggled on both outings this term.
runners.comment: On a competitive mark but beaten on last 20 starts & he's struggled on both runs this term

runners.name: Exposure
runners.spotlight: 4yo who failed to progress in France in the second half of last year but this two-time winner has joined a top yard for his British career and it will be no surprise to see a big performance on his stable debut.
runners.comment: Two-time French winner who has joined a top yard for his British career

runners.name: Sterling Knight
runners.spotlight: Disappointing at Epsom a fortnight ago but won on the other Newmarket course (1m, good) last month and he has a very solid record at this track, featuring a C&D win and 2l fourth in this last year; could bounce back.
runners.comment: Disappointing at Epsom last time but has a very solid record here and could bounce back

runners.name: Safe Harbor
runners.spotlight: A six-race maiden but has finished a close second in both races at about 1m since cheekpieces were fitted and remains on a competitive mark; turn looks near.
runners.comment: Has finished a close second in both races at about 1m since cheekpieces were fitted

runners.name: Guest House
runners.spotlight: Improved form to win six-runner Class 5 1m handicap at Yarmouth (good to firm) and raced too freely when upped to 1m2f off this 5lb higher mark on the Rowley Mile a month ago; back in trip and class, he's respected.
runners.comment: Improved to win a 1m handicap and raced too freely upped to 1m2f next time

runners.name: Crimson Rambler
runners.spotlight: Won twice on AW within five days in March and has perfomed just as well on turf since then; could have run better on stable debut at Sandown but this looks easier.
runners.comment: Could have run better on stable debut at Sandown but this looks easier

runners.name: Malika
runners.spotlight: Not even this low a mark looks generous on what she's shown and has raced over 1m and 1m2f; however, as a handicap debutante from her top yard she earns some respect.
runners.comment: Not even this low a mark looks generous on what she's shown

runners.name: Jamie Sommers
runners.spotlight: Exposed after ten races and has over 4l to find with Safe Harbor on their Nottingham clash 16 days ago.
runners.comment: Has over 4l to find with Safe Harbor on their Nottingham clash 16 days ago

runners.name: Mimi's Magic
runners.spotlight: 0-6; some improvement to begin with this campaign but no better for cheekpieces when behind both Safe Harbor and Jamie Sommers at Nottingham.
runners.comment: No better for cheekpieces when behind both Safe Harbor and Jamie Sommers at Nottingham

runners.name: Royal Bodyguard
runners.spotlight: Nursery winner at 1m; another good effort when third at Windsor (1m, good to firm) before perhaps unsuited by soft ground at Lingfield; enters the equation.
runners.comment: Nursery winner at two; good third at Windsor before finding soft ground against her latest

runners.name: Phantom Watch
runners.spotlight: Has run well in AW handicaps without progressing and latest run in January was a backward step; others preferred.
runners.comment: Has run well in AW handicaps without progressing and latest run was a backward step

runners.name: Crafty Blue
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Tapeta novice last autumn, but tailed in 1m handicaps this year on good to soft and the AW; had a wind operation which could turn things around.
runners.comment: Won at two; tailed off both runs this season but she has had a wind operation

runners.name: Hurricane Laughter
runners.spotlight: Some sign of ability last season over 7f and could be a different proposition now handicapping after being gelded; market useful.
runners.comment: Could be a different proposition now handicapping after being gelded; market useful

runners.name: Elvetham
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins this spring and he has continued the good work back on turf, placed at Windsor, Thirsk and Yarmouth; all ground seems to come alike to him and he holds each-way claims again now up in class.
runners.comment: Two AW wins this year; placed in each of his three turf runs this summer; each-way shout

runners.name: Grand Citadel
runners.spotlight: Both wins were achieved in the same 0-55 handicap at Leicester 12 months apart, justifying favouritism on fast ground 25 days ago; a 4lb rise and deeper opposition can find her out today.
runners.comment: Won 0-55 handicap at Leicester for the 2nd time 25 days ago; up 4lb in a better race today

runners.name: Ancient State
runners.spotlight: Three wins, all on AW and over 5f including his stable debut in March; unplaced in both turf outings; new headgear needs to spark extra.
runners.comment: Three 5f AW wins to his name; more to prove on turf; new headgear added

runners.name: Fiscal Policy
runners.spotlight: Off the mark for the year when comfortably seeing off nine rivals at Pontefract (6f, good) 11 days ago; 2lb well in under a penalty; ran well over C&D last summer; solid claims.
runners.comment: Ready win at Pontefract 11 days ago; 2lb well in under penalty; has C&D form; solid claims
runners.owner: S Thompson, Mrs R Carr & G Shields

runners.name: Zoulu Warrior
runners.spotlight: Won two Class 6 handicaps on AW in December; not quite so good in either run this year and he returns to turf with a career-best required; Harry Vigors takes off a useful 7lb.
runners.comment: Two AW wins (6f; Class 6) in December; not quite so good in two runs this year

runners.name: Clasina
runners.spotlight: Beaten in her first 17 starts but she made a winning debut for John Berry when comfortably accounting for her rivals at Yarmouth (7f, good to firm) 299 days ago; although she has an absence to contend with, she was a non-runner for races in both April and May so is likely to be forward enough; 5lb rise and shorter trip might find her out though.
runners.comment: Off the mark on stable debut last August (7f, good to firm); up 5lb in a stronger race

runners.name: Rainbow Sorbet
runners.spotlight: No improvement for the switch to handicaps when fourth of nine at Brighton (5.3f, good to firm; visored first time) three weeks ago; headgear off today; not easily recommended.
runners.comment: Low mileage but she needs to leave her handicap debut well behind her; headgear off today

runners.name: State Of Madness
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a revival for Charlie Clover between October 2025 and February this year, winning three times; not so good in four runs for current yard but he is slipping back down the weights.
runners.comment: Good spell for C Clover between October and February; not quite so good for this yard yet
runners.owner: The Likely Lads & S & A Mares

runners.name: Spirit Of Jenny
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the eighth attempt when beating six rivals at Southwell (6f, AW) in April; second of eight at Leicester last month; should remain competitive.
runners.comment: Improving filly; not fully exposed at 6f; should remain competitive

runners.name: Jimmy Knocker
runners.spotlight: Finished fourth in the corresponding event 12 months ago and won over C&D (good to soft) in August; shaped as though the run was needed at Leicester on his comeback 25 days ago; better expected.
runners.comment: C&D winner last summer; should be sharper for his comeback; better expected today

runners.name: Rebel Path
runners.spotlight: Beat Fiscal Policy decisively on the Rowley course (6f, good to firm) last May when rated 3lb higher; ran well for third in the same race five weeks ago; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Won on Rowley course last May off 3lb higher; promising third last time; each-way shout

runners.name: Northcliff
runners.spotlight: Having tumbled down the weights he returned to form at Chepstow (7f, good to firm; well backed) three weeks ago, reportedly appreciating the return to turf; still has plenty of handicapping scope up 3lb and his new trainer (same owner) has won with two of his last three runners; visor returns today having worn cheekpieces last time.
runners.comment: Landed gamble at Chepstow (7f) last month; 3lb rise fair; visor back for stable debut

runners.name: Havana Sky
runners.spotlight: Prolific AW winner and has a fast-ground turf success to his name too; on a winning mark but others may have his measure this time.
runners.comment: Prolific AW winner; has won on turf but others may have his measure back on grass

runners.name: Currahee
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 7f when joining Stuart Williams last summer and he also ran well over 6f at Chelmsford; absent since a lesser effort at Leicester last September; stable also runs Rebel Path.
runners.comment: Two 7f wins for Stuart Williams last summer; absent since a poor run last September
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Spring Bloom
runners.spotlight: Four runs for John Butler, running well in three Class 5s but below par in a Class 3 in May; goes well at the Newmarket tracks and he holds each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Conditions no problem and arrives in form; shouldn't be too far away

runners.name: Mart
runners.spotlight: Solid in-frame efforts in his last two turf runs, the latest when third of nine at Yarmouth (6f, soft; for Zac Lloyd) nine days ago; more minor money perhaps his best hope.
runners.comment: Two solid efforts since returned to turf; needs more to take this

runners.name: Way To Dubai
runners.spotlight: Only 1lb higher than for his Lingfield (1m, AW) in January; up and down since that success and could prove vulnerable back sprinting.
runners.comment: On a fair mark but it has been a mixed bag since his 1m AW win in January; others stronger

runners.name: Ancient Times
runners.spotlight: In fine form in the first half of 2025 but he ended that campaign with a couple of low-key efforts; can go well fresh and he returns off a dangerous mark so he is worth a market check.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 quietly but dropped in the weights as a result; can go well fresh; check market

runners.name: Tradewinds
runners.spotlight: Built upon his modest Navan debut when beaten only 2l in a 6f Curragh maiden behind two colts bound for Royal Ascot; showed plenty of speed that day and was only collered 1f out so this trip should be fine; the one to beat today.
runners.comment: Beaten only 2l in a 6f Curragh maiden when showed plenty of speed; the one to beat today

runners.name: Bit Of A Buzz
runners.spotlight: Similar efforts in defeat in three maidens over 5f-6f, with marginally best effort at Fairyhouse last week over 6f; likely to be on the premises again but vulnerable to a smart sort.
runners.comment: Best effort at Fairyhouse last week over 6f; likely to be on the premises again

runners.name: Quinn Blue
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Prince X J (RPR 80); dam 1m winner (75), out of 7f 2yo winner; half-brother was a useful winner for this yard; finished fourth in a Naas barrier trial recently; considered so check the market.
runners.comment: Finished fourth in a Naas barrier trial recently; considered so check the market

runners.name: Liteman
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to Australian 7f/1m winner Baltic Coast; dam Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner (RPR 93), half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Rajasinghe; unsold for E4,500 as a yearling; likely best watched on debut unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Likely best watched on debut unless the market suggests otherwise

runners.name: Edels Charm
runners.spotlight: Cost 4,500euros as a yearling; first foal; dam 1m AW winner (RPR 66), half-sister to winners Panama (6f/6.5f 2yo inc US) and Maybury (KSA 1m); yard is 3-12 with its Irish 2yo runners this season so worth a second look on debut.
runners.comment: Yard is 3-12 with its Irish 2yo runners this season so worth a second look on debut

runners.name: Magical Moonlight
runners.spotlight: Beaten 9l on debut at Fairyhouse over 6f but showed speed until tiring late; the drop to this trip could suit and should have improved since so can do better.
runners.comment: Showed speed until tiring over 6f on debut; the drop to this trip could suit;  do better

runners.name: Best Love
runners.spotlight: Beaten 7.5l on debut in a Curragh maiden behind a rival who has since been Group-placed; should have taken a step forward from that and go closer today.
runners.comment: Beaten 7.5l on debut in a Curragh maiden; should have taken a step forward; go closer here

runners.name: Shafters Legacy
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two maidens at Cork and will be of more interest in nurseries.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two maidens at Cork and will be of more interest in nurseries

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap

runners.name: Treasured Royal
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Navan maiden on second start last September and has run well at times on strong company since; beaten 5l by Sarahmae at Cork last time over this trip and has work to do even on better terms.
runners.comment: Beaten 5l by Sarahmae at Cork last time over this trip; work to do even on better terms

runners.name: Mary Shoelaces
runners.spotlight: Won for the third time (all over 5f) when scoring at the Curragh last October off a 5lb lower mark; well below that level on seasonal debut but can come on for that; has run well in defeat here.
runners.comment: Below her best on seasonal debut but can come on for that; has run well in defeat here

runners.name: Sarahmae
runners.spotlight: Won for the fourth time (all over 5f) when comfortably scoring at Cork five days ago; raised 10lb though has been competitive off similar marks in the past; a big player if in the same form on a quick turnaround.
runners.comment: Won at Cork 5 days ago; up 10lb but a big player if in the same form on a quick turnaround
runners.owner: Mary B Hogan & Michael M O'Brien

runners.name: Shadow Of The Moon
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D maiden on third start last year; well below that form in two starts this year but a return to this venue might re-ignite a spark so not ruled out.
runners.comment: C&D winner last year; below that form in two starts this year; returning to here may help

runners.name: Two And Two
runners.spotlight: Sprang a 40/1 surprise when getting off the mark at Dundalk in January over 6f; solid Curragh handicap run next time but failed to stay 1m when poor last time; could rebound so one to consider.
runners.comment: Failed to stay 1m when poor last time; could rebound so one to consider

runners.name: Daneh Of Dandy
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained in UK; won twice as a 2yo (5f-6f) and ran creditably in defeat in group and stakes company; not a bad effort on seasonal debut but was poor last time; since sold for 23,000gns; check the market on stable debut.
runners.comment: Smart 2yo ran poorly last time and changed hands since; check the market on stable debut

runners.name: Bel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Well beaten lately and is 23lb out of the handicap so can't be entertained.
runners.comment: Well beaten lately and is 23lb out of the handicap so can't be entertained
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: Spirit Above
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form to take second over C&D last time, just denied by Cashel Blue after making most of the running; is well-drawn to lead again and should make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Just denied by Cashel Blue over C&D last time and should make another bold bid

runners.name: Baby Basma
runners.spotlight: Went missing after winning a Cork maiden over this trip on debut in April 2025; two AW runs in the spring were below that level; questions to answer with cheekpieces on back to turf.
runners.comment: Below best in two AW runs this spring; questions to answer with cheekpieces back to turf

runners.name: Cashel Blue
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form to get off the mark on handicap debut over C&D last time, beating Spirit Above a neck; was found to have blood at both nostrils post-race but if none the worse for that should go well off 6lb higher.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last time; had blood at both nostrils post-race but a player nonetheless

runners.name: Aughlish
runners.spotlight: Placed in three maidens over 5f-6f in the spring; soft ground was against her at Sligo but was then disappointing back at Dundalk last time; blinkers need to work for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Disappointing back at Dundalk last time; blinkers need to work for handicap debut

runners.name: This Guy
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 15 starts but has been placed several times; ran well enough behind Cashel Blue over C&D last time but seemed to find this test a bit sharp; has to do more.
runners.comment: Ran well enough behind Cashel Blue over C&D last time but seemed to find this a bit sharp

runners.name: Mini Cotai
runners.spotlight: Maiden hasn't been beaten far at times and the drop back to 5f should suit but is racing from 7lb out of the handicap so needs a fair bit more.
runners.comment: Maiden is racing from 7lb out of the handicap so needs a fair bit more

runners.name: Gonna Be Golden
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f maiden at Dundalk last December; had excuses on next two starts but was well beaten over 6.5f at the Curragh last time; the drop to 5f with a tongue-tie added will need to re-ignite the spark.
runners.comment: Well beaten last time; the drop to 5f with a tongue-tie added needs to re-ignite the spark

runners.name: The Right One
runners.spotlight: Hit the frame in five of seven turf maidens over 5f-6f last year including over C&D; was poor in a Dundalk nursery on final start; however, is one to consider on return with cheekpieces added, especially if strong in the market.
runners.comment: One to consider on return with cheekpieces added, especially if strong in the market

runners.name: The Love Machine
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f Navan nursery last year for Mick Mulvany; has been well below that level for this yard in 2026 and isn't easy to fancy despite dropping plenty in the weights.
runners.comment: In poor form and isn't easy to fancy despite dropping plenty in the weights

races.race_name: Ampion Electrical Test And Solar Handicap

runners.name: She's A Gift
runners.spotlight: Winless from 16 but her best effort came on the AW at Wolverhampton last time when beaten a neck (5f); overall record suggests she might struggle to back it up back on turf in a better quality event.
runners.comment: Best effort came latest when beaten a neck on AW; may struggle to back it up

runners.name: Shamanka
runners.spotlight: Well held on handicap debut in final start for Natalia Lupini and no better on stable debut/seasonal return at the Curragh (6.5f, yielding); the cheekpieces are new.
runners.comment: Well held on h'cap bow and no better on stable debut/seasonal return

runners.name: Arctic Steps
runners.spotlight: Took his record to 2-10 on turf when successful at Naas off 68 in May (5f, good); moderate effort on AW at Dundalk last time (5f), behind Clonmacash; could go well back on this surface.
runners.comment: Took record on turf to 2-10 when winning at Naas; poor AW run latest; chance

runners.name: Jordan Breeze
runners.spotlight: Built on runner-up finishes over 5f at Dundalk and Bellewstown (soft) to score at Cork (soft); never dangerous off revised mark at Navan next time (5f, good); possibly best with a soft surface so watch the weather.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice this season before winning at Cork; never involved latest; prefers soft

runners.name: Collective Power
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner and three on turf, including this race in 2022 off 67; close third at Dundalk in April (6f) and took second over 5f there on penultimate; two turf runs this season have not been at the same level, including when fifth of nine at Navan last time (6f, good); 8lb lower mark on turf now.
runners.comment: Better on AW but has three turf wins inc' this race in 2022; two turf runs this term poor
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs S Power

runners.name: Verified
runners.spotlight: A 22-race maiden but has occasionally shown winning potential; those instances haven't come this season for his new trainer though; others more likely.
runners.comment: 22-race m'den; poor in three runs for new yard this term; others more likely

runners.name: Clonmacash
runners.spotlight: Much stronger record on AW (5-30, placed nine times) as opposed to turf (1-23, placed four times); despite that, his run at Navan over 6f last time was one of his better efforts (6f, good); chance if building on that.
runners.comment: Stronger record on AW although his run at Navan last time was one of his better efforts

runners.name: Skillman Ave
runners.spotlight: Is 1-24 with the win coming at Tipperary last season (5f, good) off 68; mark in freefall since but no sign of a revival; 10lb wrong.
runners.comment: Won last season off 68 but mark in freefall since and no sign of revival; 10lb wrong

runners.name: Lethimfly
runners.spotlight: A 5f maiden winner at two; has run three times this season; good second at Dundalk over 5f before poor efforts back there next time and back on turf at Navan; others preferred.
runners.comment: Won a m'den at two; poor in two of three runs this season; others preferred

runners.name: Spirit Of Eagles
runners.spotlight: Bridged a gap of almost three years between wins when landing a Fairyhouse claimer (6f, good) last time, beating some much higher rated rivals; only other win came over C&D; up 19lb for her latest win which dampens enthusiasm.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2023 added to that when landing a claimer last time; up 19lb for that

runners.name: Nouvel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f Cork handicap last season (good to yielding); okay run in same race last time when fourth; overall form this season has been underwhelming; down 2lb now.
runners.comment: Overall form this season has been underwhelming; down 2lb since fourth latest
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Median Auction Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Joga Bonito
runners.spotlight: Made 85,000euros as a yearling; beaten favourite in both starts but good runs all the same; showed speed but green when fourth of ten at Roscommon (7.5f, good); dropped back to 6f at Fairyhouse when taking the runner-up spot behind an easy winner; shaped as though return to this trip would help last time; big player.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite in both starts but good runs; return to this trip should suit; big player

runners.name: Closefield Beau
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to 7f AW winner Dandy G Boy (RPR 73); dam 6f/7f winner (inc 2yo; 99), half-sister to winners Ballylemon (1m4f) and Inaad (1m), out of unraced half-sister to 2m2f Group 2 winner Honolulu; check market.
runners.comment: Half-brother to 7f AW winner (RPR 73); check market

runners.name: Kraar
runners.spotlight: Finished a short head in front of Shesthedevil on debut at Listowel (6.5f, good); needs to improve to win.
runners.comment: Short head ahead of Shesthedevil on debut; improvement needed to win

runners.name: Golden Arizona
runners.spotlight: Showed speed but green under pressure when beating just one rival home at the Curragh (6f, good); beaten 13l over 7f at Gowran last time (7f, soft); needs to step forward to be in contention.
runners.comment: Needs to step forward from moderate opening runs to be in contention

runners.name: Spanish View
runners.spotlight: 3,000euros yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Nice Spirit (French 1m; RPR 83) and Artic Power (US 6f turf); dam placed 1m in France (78), half-sister to 1m2f Group 2 winner Hanalia and 1m Group 3 winners Hamariyna and Hunaina; others more appealing.
runners.comment: 3,000 euros yearling; half-sister to two winners but others more appealing

runners.name: Supreme Act
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros foal and purchased for 21,000euros as a yearling; third foal; half-brother to Italian 5f-1m winner Rosa Dei Venti (inc 2yo); dam maiden (stayed 1m2f; RPR 71), half-sister to winners Bid Adieu (useful 1m2f-12.5f), Sparkle Factor (7f/1m) and Rock Sound (6f/7f); trainer 0-4 with 2yo runners this term.
runners.comment: 21,000euros yearling; half-brother to Italian 5f-1m winner (inc 2yo)

runners.name: Full Volume
runners.spotlight: Lucky Vega colt; dam 9.4f winner (RPR 88), out of 7f Group 3 winning sister to 1m4f Listed winner Ektihaam and Listed-placed 1m-1m3f winner Onlyhuman; interesting debutant with trainer 3-18 with 2yos this term.
runners.comment: Dam 9.4f winner (RPR 88), out of 7f Group 3 winner; interesting debutant

runners.name: High Morals
runners.spotlight: A 90,000 euros yearling was sent off the 3-1 favourite for his debut at the Curragh (6f, good) but weakened tamely into eighth; likely capable of better.
runners.comment: 90,000euros yearling was fav' on debut but weakened tamely; capable of more probably

runners.name: Noble Agenda
runners.spotlight: 4,000euros foal was bought for 25,000euros as a yearling; tenth foal; half-brother to winners Sand Diego (5f 2yo; RPR 77), Supreme Machine (1m2f; 73), Relief Quest (French 1m/1m1f 2yo) and Ne Jamais Renoncer (French 1m2f 2yo); dam 5f winner (80); worth a market check.
runners.comment: Cheap foal was bought for 25,000euros as a yearling; half-brother to multiple winners

runners.name: Sophia Royale
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival over 5f at Cork on debut (soft); slight improvement back there over 6f last time (soft); cheekpieces on now.
runners.comment: Slight improvement over debut over 6f at Cork last time; cheekpieces on now

runners.name: The Pipeman
runners.spotlight: Cotai Glory colt; 6,000euros yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to winner in Kazakhstan; dam Italian 7f/1m winner, half-sister to winners Gentlemen Only (HK 1m Group 1) and Dee Dee D'Oro (Italian 1m2f Listed); others appeal more on paper.
runners.comment: Cotai Glory colt; half-brother to winner in Kazakhstan; others appeal more on paper

runners.name: Mutamarid Lsk
runners.spotlight: Ten Sovereigns colt; dam 1m AW 2yo/Qatari 1m2f winner (RPR 86), closely related to Scandinavian 1m 2yo-1m7f winner Zakopane, half-sister to winners Admiral De Vega (Swedish 1m1f Group 3) and Zeeyara (French 1m2f Listed); seventh of nine in a Naas barrier trial in May
runners.comment: Seventh of nine in a Naas barrier trial, which doesn't augur well

runners.name: Pink Venom
runners.spotlight: First reserve; 13.5l behind in seventh at Leopardstown earlier this month (7f, soft, 80-1); others more likely.
runners.comment: Reserve; 13.5l seventh of ten on debut (80-1); others more likely

runners.name: Caoch The Piper
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; moderate effort at Leopardstown when sixth of eight (7f, soft); more needed.
runners.comment: Reserve; moderate effort at Leopardstown when sixth of eight; more needed

runners.name: Oristown Boy
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; well beaten on debut at Fairyhouse (6f, good) when stumbling badly after the start; others preferred.
runners.comment: Reserve; well beaten on debut when stumbling; others preferred
runners.owner: Ms Lisa Sheridan & Patrick M O'Brien & Larry Murray

runners.name: Shesthedevil
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on debut over 6.5f at Listowel in a first-time hood (now left off); needs to improve a good bit.
runners.comment: Well beaten on debut at Listowel; needs to improve a good bit

runners.name: Portaliffe Phil
runners.spotlight: Has beaten just two rivals from two starts; unlikely.
runners.comment: Has beaten just two rivals from two starts; unlikely

runners.name: Treasure Rose
runners.spotlight: A five-time runner-up from nine starts, the latest of which came last time at Killarney (1m, good, 6-5) when beaten a neck; cheekpieces go on for her handicap debut and her consistency should pay off somewhere soon.
runners.comment: Five-time runner-up from nine starts; makes h'cap debut and consistency will pay soon

runners.name: Royal Saxo
runners.spotlight: Seldom ran a bad race when trained in France, with five wins and ten places from 20 starts; that consistent record was nowhere to be seen on stable debut at Limerick when last of nine runners (1m, yielding) at 66-1; best form is over further.
runners.comment: Seldom ran a bad race in France but last of nine on stable debut latest

runners.name: Summer Island
runners.spotlight: AW winner in February on stable debut (1m); stepped up from Bellewstown third when winning readily at the Curragh next time (7f, good); another creditable effort off 9lb higher back at the Curragh next time when second of 19 behind an easy winner; should go well again.
runners.comment: Ready winner at Curragh and good second off this mark last time; should go well
runners.owner: Nigel O'Hare & Martin W White & Mark Devlin & C G Bryson

runners.name: Thurso
runners.spotlight: Three wins last year, at 6f and 7f; ground was firm when signing off with success for Richard Hughes at Bath (5.5f, firm); respectable fourth on stable debut at Navan last time (5f, good) when shaping as though a return to further would suit; in the mix but won't want ground too soft.
runners.comment: Four-time British winner was a respectable fourth on stable debut; trip should suit

runners.name: Little Empire
runners.spotlight: Admirably consistent on AW and turf; won a 7f Ayr handicap last year (good to firm); close fourth over 7f at Musselburgh in April leaving him with a turf record of 2-6; interesting contender with so few runs on this surface.
runners.comment: Admirably consistent with 2-6 record on turf; close fourth last time; interesting

runners.name: Glyndwr
runners.spotlight: Won three of four races at Dundalk, signing off his tenure with Ger Lyons with back-to-back victories over 1m there; ran no race on stable debut/seasonal return at Leopardstown (1m2f, good); much more required and may be better on AW.
runners.comment: 3-4 on AW; ran no race on seasonal return/stable debut; much more needed

runners.name: Minaun View
runners.spotlight: 0-17 and best efforts have come on AW; has found consistency on turf recently all the same; beaten just 2l into sixth over C&D here on penultimate (7f, good); soft ground probably didn't suit when 5.25l behind the winner at Leopardstown last time (7f, soft); not out of it.
runners.comment: 0-17 but has found some consistency on turf recently; beaten 2l over C&D last month

runners.name: Gotta Catch'em All
runners.spotlight: A 24-race maiden has been running respectably this season but hard to see him breaking his duck here.
runners.comment: 24-race m'den; running respectably this term but hard to see him breaking his duck
runners.owner: Mrs S Bury & Ralph Kelly & Odhran O'Neill & Thomas Cox

runners.name: Summer Snow
runners.spotlight: C&D (good) win was one of three victories last season; two efforts this term have not been his best; 2lb below his last winning mark now.
runners.comment: C&D win was one of his three victories last season; two runs this term not his best

runners.name: Imperial Dream
runners.spotlight: Close fourth over C&D in 2024 off 74; last win at the Curragh (6f, good) in August that year; has largely struggled for form since.
runners.comment: Close C&D fourth in 2024 and won at the Curragh later in year; struggled since

runners.name: Flaming Rib
runners.spotlight: Seven wins abroad; rated 113 at his peak but nothing since 2023; may come forward from seasonal return at Leopardstown (7f, soft) when fifth of ten, but he remains risky.
runners.comment: Seven wins abroad but none since 2023; may come forward from seasonal return

runners.name: Eddie G
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner but 0-12 on turf; returned from over a year off at Leopardstown (7f, soft) when beaten 14l; that doesn't provide much encouragement.
runners.comment: 0-12 on turf and ran poorly back from over a year off last time

runners.name: Battle Borne
runners.spotlight: Won a modest 7f maiden at Dundalk in February but soundly beaten in handicaps after that, including when last of all in two turf runs at Leopardstown over 1m; better effort in an AW claimer when last seen; others more likely; 12th in the 5.45 at Leopardstown yesterday.
runners.comment: Won a modest 7f AW m'den but soundly beaten since; last of all in two recent turf runs; 12th in the 5.45 at Leopardstown yesterday.

runners.name: Blues Emperor
runners.spotlight: Five wins for Johnny Murtagh; poor in opening runs for new yard until a 1l fourth of 17 at the Curragh in April (7f, soft); didn't build on that back at the Curragh last time (1m, good); 12lb below his last winning mark now.
runners.comment: Five wins for former yard but poor overall for new one; 12lb below last winning mark

runners.name: Alex Belardo
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over C&D in 2024 but has been running consistently in defeat on AW; beaten just a neck at Wolverhampton 18 days ago off 2lb lower; may build on that back at scene of his win, but with his 1-25 strike-rate caution is advised.
runners.comment: 1-25 with win coming over C&D; close second on AW latest; may build on that

runners.name: Royal County Glory
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; off the mark at Dundalk in April (7f) on AW off 3lb lower; creditable 3l fifth of 13 at Fairyhouse next time (7f, good); never better than midfield at Gowran when favourite last time (1m, yielding); claims at his best.
runners.comment: Reserve; AW winner in April off 3lb lower; claims at his best

runners.name: Miqdaad
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner in 2024; one good run last year, a 0.5l second of 13 at Limerick in July; poor return at Fairyhouse in May (6f, good); needs to improve hugely on that.
runners.comment: AW m'den winner in 2024; poor return at F'house; needs to improve hugely

runners.name: Mullacash Buzz
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner on AW has scored twice on turf; rated 7lb lower on this surface; fair fourth at Dundalk (1m) in April; weakened into sixth of 14 at Roscommon last time (7.5f, good); others preferred.
runners.comment: Rated 7lb lower on turf; fair fourth on AW in April; weakened into sixth on turf latest

runners.name: Dervo Annie
runners.spotlight: Built on good second over C&D (winner won two races back-to-back afterwards) in May to score at Fairyhouse last time (6f, good); up 6lb but rider's claim negates half of that; chance.
runners.comment: Built on good C&D second to win last time; rider's claim helps with rise; chance

runners.name: Concaire
runners.spotlight: Best effort yet on second handicap start at Limerick earlier this month (7f, yielding) when going down by a nose, despite racing keenly and wide; up 3lb but of interest once again.
runners.comment: Went down by a nose last time despite doing some things wrong; of interest again

runners.name: Roman Harry
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner is 0-27 on turf; middling effort at the Curragh last time and overall record on this surface is concerning.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner is 0-27 on turf; middling effort at the Curragh; concerning record

runners.name: Lady Kelly
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden form on soft ground; okay run at Fairyhouse on handicap debut when sixth (7f, good) in first-time blinkers (retained); improvement needed from the Irish Oaks entrant.
runners.comment: Modest m'den form; okay run on h'cap debut but improvement needed

runners.name: Stonyfalls
runners.spotlight: First reserve; soundly beaten in maidens; 66-1 for handicap debut when sixth of 14 at over C&D (good) on seasonal return; could improve.
runners.comment: Reserve; soundly beaten in m'dens; 66-1 on h'cap bow over C&D when sixth of 14

runners.name: Ukiyo
runners.spotlight: 1-13 with the win coming at Cork in September (6f, yielding) off 60; some positive placed efforts this term at Navan (6f, heavy), Listowel (7f, good) and most recently at Fairyhouse (6f, good); latest run can be marked up because slowly away and was waiting for a gap in the straight; in the mix.
runners.comment: Won at Cork off 6lb higher last season; good placed efforts this term inc' latest

runners.name: Blue Anthem
runners.spotlight: Three wins for George Boughey (two on AW); five outings for new yard have been disappointing but he drops into 0-60 company for the first time now (has won off 70), so can't be dismissed.
runners.comment: Poor form for new yard but drops in grade now (won off 70 in past); not dismissed

runners.name: Brains
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; veteran shaped nicely in his opening two runs this season over 7f at Leopardstown (yielding to soft) and an extended 7f at Roscommon (good); a bit below that level at Roscommon last time (1m, yielding); 1lb below winning mark last season.
runners.comment: Reserve; veteran shaped nicely in opening two runs but a bit below that latest

runners.name: Toy Soldier
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden and a claimer in Britain; poor form for new yard until 2l fourth of 15 at Killarney in May; attractive mark if back in form.
runners.comment: In poor form for new yard until 2l fourth last time; attractive mark if back in form

runners.name: Rock Etoile
runners.spotlight: Third-place finish at Gowran was a rare positive recent effort (1m, yielding) on penultimate; didn't build on that in a similar contest there last time; three-time turf winner is well handicapped on his best form and cheekpieces go on now to try and recapture it.
runners.comment: Third at Gowran on penultimate was rare positive recent effort; well h'capped at best
runners.owner: Conor McKenna & Patrick J McKenna & Emmet McKenna

runners.name: Hot To Foxtrot
runners.spotlight: Competitive in 6f/7f handicaps on AW this season before posting a very promising third of 20 on her return to turf at the Curragh last time (6.5f, yielding); first-time cheekpieces worn that day retained; up 1lb.
runners.comment: Very promising third of 20 back on turf last time; cheekpieces worn that day retained

runners.name: Pete's Dream
runners.spotlight: M'den from 12 starts; two recent AW starts not too encouraging; others more likely.
runners.comment: M'den from 12 starts and recent AW runs not too encouraging; others more likely

runners.name: Zephron
runners.spotlight: Five wins from 52 starts but none since April 2024; weak form last season and bar one okay run on penultimate at Gowran, that trend has continued in three outings this term.
runners.comment: Five-time winner had weak form last season and that trend has continued this term

races.race_name: Listen To Track Radio Live / British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jaijai
runners.spotlight: Raced keenly before finishing sixth of ten on his debut at Ascot (6f, good) last month and stepped forward from that effort when runner-up at Hamilton (5f, good to firm; beaten around 3l) 23 days ago; sets the standard.
runners.comment: Stepped forward from his debut when runner-up over 5f at Hamilton; that sets the standard

runners.name: Cut And Sew
runners.spotlight: January foal by Starman; 260,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 7f AW 2yo/8.4f turf winner (RPR 79), sister to Italian 1m Listed winner Lord Sakay, half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Missed Call, out of maiden half-sister to 10.5f Group 3 winner Dance Dress; sole filly and she makes her debut with her yard among the winners.
runners.comment: Filly by Starman; 260,000gns yearling; dam 7f AW 2yo/8.4f turf winner; yard in fine form

runners.name: It Dunt Marra
runners.spotlight: March foal by A'Ali; £100,000 yearling; second foal; brother to 6.5f 2yo winner Fairy Oak (RPR 91); dam Listed-placed 9.4f winner (95), half-sister to useful 1m2f winner Dark Red, out of Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner; interesting newcomer and the market should be informative.
runners.comment: £100,000 yearling; brother to 6.5f 2yo winner Fairy Oak (RPR 91); interesting newcomer

runners.name: Shoof
runners.spotlight: January foal by Starman; £36,000 yearling, £90,000 2yo (breeze-ups); first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Legionario (1m Listed), Manolito (1m/8.6f) and Arth's Gold (1m4f), out of unraced half-sister to 1m4f Group 3 winner Short Pause; stable can ready a newcomer and market support should be noted.
runners.comment: January foal by Starman; £36,000 yearling, £90,000 2yo; stable can ready a newcomer

runners.name: Seathegulls
runners.spotlight: Showed some early speed before fading into seventh in the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, good to soft; 5-1) 83 days ago and unraced since; unruly in the stalls and withdrawn at Lingfield in April (9-2 at the time); gelded since and he's open to improvement.
runners.comment: Showed some early speed before finishing seventh on his debut 83 days ago; gelded since

runners.name: Boilermaker
runners.spotlight: Shaped with encouragement on his handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m2f) in April and built on that at Thirsk (1m4f, good) 34 days ago, finishing second of 11; up 3lb but he should have more to offer over this trip; major contender.
runners.comment: Built on the encouragement of handicap debut when second at Thirsk (1m4f); major contender
runners.owner: W G & A G Vestey, E Gerber And P Inglett

runners.name: Darn Hot Dame
runners.spotlight: Posted solid efforts on her first two starts in handicap company and got off the mark at the sixth attempt when winning by a short head at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) 27 days ago; up in grade off this 5lb higher mark but she could have more to offer at this sort of distance.
runners.comment: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when winning at Windsor last month; steps up in grade

runners.name: Blackisthenewblack
runners.spotlight: Had cheekpieces on for the first time when making all (dictated a steady gallop) in a 0-65 at Kempton (1m4f, AW) in April but she was disappointing on her turf debut at Thirsk (1m4f, good) last month; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Made all in first-time cheekpieces at Kempton (AW) but disappointed on turf last month
runners.owner: Team Valor, Cayton Park Stud, A Singh

runners.name: Semper Femina
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when second of eight on her debut at Kempton (1m3f, AW) in March but was well held on her next two starts; however, she ran well on her handicap debut here over 1m2f (soft) a week ago before leaving the impression she got a bit tired on the testing ground; sounder surface should suit.
runners.comment: Didn't progress in maidens but ran well on handicap debut here over 1m2f (soft) last week

runners.name: Flash Kozo
runners.spotlight: Made the running when posting a solid effort at Southwell (1m3f, AW) on his handicap debut in April but was down in the field when ridden more patiently on his turf debut at Newbury (1m2f, good) 35 days ago; steps up in trip having been dropped 2lb.
runners.comment: Solid effort on h'cap debut (AW) but down the field on turf at Newbury last time; down 2lb
runners.owner: D Bevan And Mrs D Bevan

runners.name: La Peregrina
runners.spotlight: Well held in three novice events (1m-1m3f; good to soft/AW) and he never got involved from off the pace in a race run at a modest gallop on his handicap debut at Newbury (1m2f, good) 35 days ago; steps up in trip and he may yet do better.
runners.comment: Never got involved from off the pace at Newbury (1m2f) on his handicap debut; up to 1m4f

runners.name: Aukus
runners.spotlight: Showed little on her debut but there's been more encouragement in two starts since fitted with blinkers, most recently when third of five at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) 25 days ago; had an easy enough lead on that occasion but she's open to progress now handicapping.
runners.comment: Has shown more in two starts since fitted with blinkers (1m2f/11.5f); makes handicap debut

runners.name: Celestarak
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress from his debut in novice/maiden company last year (all over 1m) and he never featured on his handicap debut/reappearance at Salisbury (1m2f, good; first start since being gelded) 27 days ago; better is needed now stepping up in trip with a hood applied.
runners.comment: Never featured on his handicap debut over 1m2f at Salisbury; steps up in trip with hood on

races.race_name: Goodwood Horseracing Club Young Persons Membership Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Analogical
runners.spotlight: Progress 4yo; won at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) in April and added to that success at Catterick (1m4f, good) 29 days ago, making all under Poppy Scott; can't be dismissed off this 2lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Progressive 4yo who made all at Catterick under Poppy Scott last time; can't be dismissed

runners.name: Galaxy Wonder
runners.spotlight: Gained his second success for this yard when winning at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in March and followed up at Bath (1m, good to soft) next time; runner-up in both starts since (1m2f) but this consistent sort still needs to prove he stays this far.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in March and Bath in April; consistent but unproven over this far

runners.name: Gallant Lion
runners.spotlight: Struggled over 1m at Southwell last month but relished the step back up in trip when winning at Brighton (1m4f, good to firm) three weeks ago; still fairly treated on his old form off this 3lb higher mark and has claims.
runners.comment: Appreciated the step back up to 1m4f when winning at Brighton last time; up 3lb; claims

runners.name: Damascus Steel
runners.spotlight: Two 1m4f AW wins for Denis Quinn in the winter and a respectable fifth at Lingfield (1m4f, AW) in April on sole starts for John Ryan; left the impression he's still in form at Thirsk 19 days ago on his first start for this yard and cheekpieces now go on.
runners.comment: Two 1m4f AW wins for Denis Quinn in the winter; fair effort on yard debut; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Masterinthewoods
runners.spotlight: Raced freely but built on his reappearance effort when winning at Salisbury (1m4f, good) 27 days ago, posting a career best; unexposed over 1m4f and he's considered off this 4lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Raced freely but that didn't stop him winning at Salisbury last time; up 4lb; considered

runners.name: Nowshesdancing
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut in a maiden at Dundalk (10.5f, AW) for Mark Fahey in January; creditable stable debut at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm) in April but she was well held on her handicap debut at Leicester (1m2f, good) 11 days ago; steps up in trip with better needed.
runners.comment: Won at Dundalk on his debut; third on his stable debut but well held in a h'cap latest

runners.name: Zikany
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin winner here on heavy ground over 2m in October 2024 and followed up with a smooth success at Newbury (2m, heavy) next time; a beaten favourite at Nottingham (soft) 590 days ago and it's likely he'll need his first start since over a trip short of his best.
runners.comment: Won twice on heavy ground over 2m in October 2024; first start for 590 days likely needed

runners.name: Koala Rose
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise on her debut at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) in December and built on that when third of five on her reappearance at Newmarket (1m4f, good) 34 days ago; may come on for that and she has good claims if seeing out this longer trip.
runners.comment: Built on her debut when third at Newmarket (1m4f) on her return; steps up in trip; claims

runners.name: Treble Nine
runners.spotlight: Dam unraced sister to 1m3f Listed winner/Derby runner-up Khalifa Sat; showed plenty when runner-up to a stablemate with more experience on his debut at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) 25 days ago and is open to progress over this longer trip; stable has won the last two renewals of this and there's lots to like.
runners.comment: Showed plenty on his debut at Windsor last month; stable won the last two renewals of this

runners.name: Grilse
runners.spotlight: Always behind in a Newbury maiden (1m3f, good) in April and he was beaten 81l when last of five at Newmarket (1m4f, good) 34 days ago; steps up in trip in first-time cheekpieces; others preferred.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two starts at Newbury (1m3f) and Newmarket (1m4f); cheekpieces applied
runners.owner: Mrs Valda Burke And Mrs Belinda Miles

runners.name: Phantom Recon
runners.spotlight: 340,000euros yearling; 50-1 on his debut at Windsor (1m2f, good to soft) 11 days ago but showed plenty of ability, finishing fourth of 14 despite running green; plenty of stamina in his pedigree and now steps up in trip with improvement possible.
runners.comment: 50-1 on his debut at Windsor (1m2f) but showed ability; plenty of stamina in his pedigree

runners.name: Make A Splash
runners.spotlight: Filly by Cracksman; thirteenth foal; half-sister to winners Thomas Hobson (1m2f-2m4f Flat including Group 2, RPR 116; smart 2m-2m5f hurdle), La Superba (1m/8.4f; 93) and Fearless (bumper and 2m hurdle/chase); dam 8.6f AW winner (94), half-sister to 1m Listed winner Brindisi; plenty of stamina in her pedigree and she's one to keep an eye on in the betting on her debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to winners including Thomas Hobson (1m2f-2m4f Flat); one to keep an eye on

runners.name: Sixty Plus
runners.spotlight: Completed a four-timer over hurdles when scoring at Worcester (2m4f, good) last September; below his best since (pulled up last time) and has the visor back on instead of cheekpieces for this first start on the Flat.
runners.comment: Below his best since winning at Worcester last September; pulled up latest; Flat debut

runners.name: Crystal Jet
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper at Hereford (good; first-time cheekpieces) in April but was a 16l fourth of 13 in a maiden hurdle at Warwick 46 days ago; her dam won a Listed event on the Flat and this filly now makes her debut in this sphere.
runners.comment: Won a bumper at Hereford in April (first-time cheekpieces); now makes her Flat debut

runners.name: Pacific Storm
runners.spotlight: Dam French 1m2f-1m7f winner (including Group 3; 109); slowly into stride and looked clueless on his debut at Salisbury (1m2f, good; 8-1) last month, beating only one of his five rivals home; bred to be suited by this longer trip but a big step forward is required.
runners.comment: Looked clueless on his debut at Salisbury (1m2f); up in trip with much better needed

runners.name: Tinkety Tonk
runners.spotlight: Dam 1m2f-2m2f winner (including AW/Cesarewitch/Group 3; 107); failed to beat a rival home after missing the break on his debut at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm; 12-1) last month and now steps up 4f in trip; likely has better to come but a big step forward is needed.
runners.comment: Dam won the 2016 Cesarewitch; last of 12 after missing the break on his debut over 1m2f

runners.name: Authorised Speed
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner (good to soft) and three-time winner over hurdles (soft); absent since finishing fifth in a beginners' chase at Warwick in May 2024 and now makes his belated Flat debut (dam French 1m2f-12.5f winner); market should be informative.
runners.comment: Three-time winner over hurdles; absent since running over fences in May 2024; Flat debut

runners.name: Maestro Del Mar
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two starts on dirt in Meydan (1m1f/7f); fourth at San Siro (1m1f, good) in October on his first start for Antonio Marcialis but beaten a long way in the Italian St Leger (1m7f, heavy) when last seen 209 days ago; betting should help guide on his stable/British debut.
runners.comment: 0-4 (7f-1m7f); last seen tailing off in the Italian St Leger in November; yard debut

runners.name: Get Outta Here
runners.spotlight: Showed improved form when third of nine in a novice at Epsom (8.5f, good) in April and took another step forward on his handicap debut at Lingfield (7.5f) 20 days ago, coming from the back of the field to win a shade cosily; raised 6lb for that success but looks the type to continue to progress.
runners.comment: Won a shade cosily on his h'cap debut at Lingfield and should have more to offer; up 6lb

runners.name: See Blue
runners.spotlight: Got up late to win at Kempton (1m, AW) on his debut in September and ran to a similar level under a penalty at Southwell next time; beaten a head at Haydock (1m, good to firm) on his turf/handicap debut 55 days ago and has since been gelded; open to further progress.
runners.comment: AW winner on his debut; beaten a head on his h'cap debut 55 days ago; since been gelded

runners.name: Abundant
runners.spotlight: Made all when winning at Southwell (1m, AW) in October on his third start and posted a solid effort at Windsor (1m, good to firm; first start since being gelded) on his handicap debut last month; may come on for that first outing for 199 days and holds claims.
runners.comment: Won a 1m maiden at Southwell in October; solid effort at Windsor on his reappearance

runners.name: Annastarzy
runners.spotlight: Didn't have the best of passages when eighth of 12 on her handicap debut at Windsor (1m, good to firm) and posted an improved effort to win at Thirsk (1m, good) 19 days ago; now 3lb higher but there could be more to come after just six starts.
runners.comment: Posted an improved effort when winning at Thirsk last time; should have more to offer

runners.name: Euston Hall
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the third attempt when winning a Yarmouth maiden (1m, good to firm) in April and followed up on his handicap debut at Newbury (1m, good; made virtually all) 34 days ago; that form has since been franked by the runner-up and he's respected off this 6lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Made a winning handicap debut at Newbury and that forms been franked by the second; up 6lb

runners.name: Mr Writer
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a handicap at Lingfield (7f, good to firm) last month and got off the mark at the eighth attempt when landing a steadily run maiden at Catterick (7f) 20 days ago; steps up in trip (his brother stays a 1m) and has place claims returned to a handicap.
runners.comment: Off the mark at the eighth attempt when winning a 7f maiden last time; steps up in trip

runners.name: Ciarrai Abu
runners.spotlight: 100-1 when sixth at the Curragh (7f, good) on his debut last year and came clear of the third when runner-up at Brighton (1m, good to soft) in October on his first start for this yard; made a winning reappearance over this C&D (good) 48 days ago and now makes switches to handicaps; could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Made a winning reappearance in a C&D maiden last month; now switches to handicap company

runners.name: Queen Tamara
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Chepstow maiden last August and went close on her handicap debut at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) next time, form that has been well franked by the winner; bred to be suited by this step up to 1m but she needs to leave last month's disappointing reappearance effort well behind.
runners.comment: Went close on her handicap debut and that form has been franked; well beaten on her return

runners.name: Politely
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Lingfield (1m, AW) by 5l in March and made it 2-2 on the AW when following up on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW); however, she was held back on grass at Ascot (1m, good) last month and has a little bit to prove on turf, albeit this is a drop in grade; stable won this last year.
runners.comment: 2-2 on the AW (1m/8.5f) but was held on grass at Ascot last month; may be better on the AW

runners.name: Sponsor
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied for all four starts; showed ability in each of his three starts in novice/maiden company, including when third of 15 at Newbury (7f, good) in a race won by a nice prospect who has since franked the form, but he never featured in the Silver Bowl at Carlisle (1m, good to firm) on his handicap debut; down in grade.
runners.comment: Third at Newbury reads well but never featured on his h'cap debut; drops in grade
runners.owner: Al Shaqab Racing, Miller And Day

runners.name: Carefree Dream
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 12 starts came on soft ground at Bath (5f) in September and she returned to form following some lesser efforts when beaten a neck over this C&D (soft) a week ago; this likely quicker ground probably isn't ideal.
runners.comment: Sole win came on soft and beaten a neck on soft over C&D a week ago; ground a concern

runners.name: Red Snapper
runners.spotlight: Has been mostly in good form since cheekpieces were applied, winning on heavy ground at Bath in October and finishing runner-up on three occasions (good/good to firm); back up in grade but has each-way claims.
runners.comment: Has been mostly in good form since the cheekpieces were applied; up in grade; ew claims

runners.name: Akirra
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; runner-up on quick ground at Windsor (latterly promoted to first) last August and he bolted up when a short-priced favourite in a maiden at Wolverhampton in November; below his best on his final start last year and left the impression his first start for 178 days was needed when behind Havana Smile at Sandown (first start since being gelded) 22 days ago.
runners.comment: Behind Havana Smile at Sandown latest but probably needed that first start for 178 days

runners.name: Havana Smile
runners.spotlight: Stepped forward from his reappearance over 7f when dropped to this trip at Sandown (good) 22 days ago, coming through against the rail to gain his first success; up 5lb but that was just his second try over 5f, and there could be more to come.
runners.comment: Gained his first success when dropped to this trip at Sandown last time; now 5lb higher

runners.name: Too Darn Good
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but he was third of nine at Kempton (6f, AW) last October; failed to build on his stable debut when down the field at Bath (5.5f, good to firm) 28 days ago and cheekpieces are now reached for.
runners.comment: 0-8; failed to build on his stable debut last time and cheekpieces are now reached for
runners.owner: K Hunter, D Leon And Hunscote Stud Ltd

runners.name: Kesta
runners.spotlight: Won three times at two, including at Nottingham (6f, good to soft) in October; however, he's struggled in three starts since (two this year) and drops back in trip with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Won at Nottingham (6f) in October but struggled in three starts since; has a bit to prove

runners.name: One And Gone
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 13 starts came in a novice at Kempton (6f, AW) in November on his stable debut; has posted some solid efforts since, including when fourth over this C&D last month, and can have his latest run ignored having stumbled early on; can bounce back.
runners.comment: 1-13; fourth over this C&D last month and had an excuse at Epsom two weeks ago (stumbled)

runners.name: Duskaura
runners.spotlight: No impact in two Listed races after winning a fillies' maiden at Ripon (5f, good) for Archie Watson last June; hasn't threatened in three starts on the AW since joining this stable and has been dropped 2lb since last seen 80 days ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Won a fillies' maiden at Ripon (5f, good) for Archie Watson; yet to threaten for this yard

races.race_name: Magners Original Cider Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Stoneacre Joe
runners.spotlight: Recorded RPRs in the 70s when runner-up on first two starts this season (both 6f, good to firm) and will be hard to beat if somewhere near that level today but was well beaten (albeit on soft ground) on last month's handicap debut and managed only fourth of six when warm favourite for 5f Catterick novice three weeks ago; now has a point to prove.
runners.comment: Began season with two creditable seconds but now has a point to prove

runners.name: Star With Purpose
runners.spotlight: Displayed promise on all three starts, most recently when third in first-time blinkers (retained) at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) last month; ought to play a major role here.
runners.comment: Good third at Pontefract last month and ought to play a significant role here

runners.name: Sand Sea
runners.spotlight: Made quite promising debut at Catterick (6f, good to soft) just over a year ago; not seen again until never-dangerous seventh at Thirsk (7f, good) a fortnight ago but has possibilities if that run brought him on.
runners.comment: Made quite promising debut in May 2025; not seen again until lesser effort this month

runners.name: Shatin Beauty
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister to three Italian winners; dam unraced half-sister to Coventry Stakes third Saeiqa; probably best watched on debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to three Italian winners probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Tide Runner
runners.spotlight: Third of five in Beverley seller (5f, good to firm) on last month's debut; that form isn't anything to go wild about but he has obvious scope for improvement and shaped very much as though this extra furlong will suit.
runners.comment: Third of five in 5f seller last month; can improve over this longer trip; a possible

runners.name: Le Puy
runners.spotlight: Bettered inauspicious 2yo debut (6f) when fourth of six at Catterick (7f, good) in April but needs to take another step forward here.
runners.comment: Ran okay when fourth of six on seasonal debut (7f) but improvement is required here

runners.name: Brash
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability when seventh on both starts (6f/7f; very slowly away on debut) but has a bit to find here.
runners.comment: Displayed ability when seventh on both starts (6f/7f) but has a bit to find here

runners.name: Alpha Platoon
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to smart sprinter Ain't Nobody; in rear throughout when a big-priced outsider for recent debut at Catterick (6f, good to soft).
runners.comment: In rear throughout when a big-priced outsider for recent debut at Catterick (6f)

runners.name: Thistle Nil
runners.spotlight: A well-beaten outsider on both starts (6f/1m1f); can't be recommended.
runners.comment: A well-beaten outsider on both starts (6f/1m1f); can't be recommended

runners.name: Glasses Up
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has gained 11 of his 12 wins here (nine over this trip); safely held at other tracks on last two outings but now 2lb lower than when close third here (1m1f, good to firm) in early May; pretty versatile regarding ground; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Well held on last two outings but this 11-time course winner returns here on workable mark

runners.name: Myrrh
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage filly; needs to prove her wellbeing after weakening quickly at Thirsk a fortnight ago but is now 6lb lower than when placed on seasonal debut at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) in April, and won't be bothered by the forecast rain (gained her 2yo win on soft ground).
runners.comment: Low-mileage filly; not herself last time but can be dangerous off this mark if on song

runners.name: Off Spin
runners.spotlight: Posted sound effort when third of 11 at Pontefract (1m2f, good) 11 days ago and competes off the same mark here but he's 0-15 now and not yet proven on ground slower than good.
runners.comment: Placed off this mark 11 days ago but 0-15 now and not yet proven on softish ground
runners.owner: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor

runners.name: Fans Favourite
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to open account at Lingfield (1m1f, soft) a fortnight ago, his sixth start; will be favoured by the forecast rain here and might have more to offer over the slightly longer trip; high on the list.
runners.comment: Scored on soft ground at Lingfield this month and will be favoured by forecast rain here

runners.name: Dingwall
runners.spotlight: Runner-up three consecutive times on AW in January-March and also when back on turf here (1m1f, good to soft) last month; needs to up his game after some below-par efforts since, though, and will have something to prove if the ground is slower than good.
runners.comment: Second four times in a row this year but not at best lately and recent rain not helpful

runners.name: Penelope's Sister
runners.spotlight: Always behind on all three appearances this season but was a three-time course winner (1m, good to firm-soft) last year, making all on two occasions and ridden prominently on the other; fine over today's trip and sure to appreciate this ease in grade; interesting to see whether connections opt to revert to more positive tactics today.
runners.comment: Three course wins last year (made all twice); always behind all three runs this term

runners.name: Tap Dancer
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on either start for new stable this season, latterly when fourth of eight here (1m, good to firm) a month ago; will be suited by this move back up in trip and significant rain will make this heavy-ground winner of strong interest.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on either start for new stable; significant rain will aid his cause

runners.name: Elizabeth Bay
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden who ended last season on a low note and was also well beaten on first two outings this year (7f/1m); moves up in trip with a lot to prove.
runners.comment: Well beaten on both starts this year (7f/1m); up in trip with a lot to prove

runners.name: Shifter
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner (1m/1m2f); not the force of old but returned from break with two respectable runs last month, the latter a fourth of 12 at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm; also suited by soft); probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Not the force of old but returned from break with two respectable efforts last month

runners.name: Zebra Star
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D (good to soft) off 3lb higher last summer and returns here after respectable fifth at Musselburgh (1m, good) this month but has a modest strike-rate and a steadily regressive profile; others preferred.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last summer but has modest strike-rate and steadily regressive profile

runners.name: Glorious Kitty
runners.spotlight: Bang-in-form filly who won from the front under Lewis Chalkley here (1m, good to firm) and at Carlisle (7f, good) last month; also effective on good to soft (unraced on soft); likely to given this a good shot.
runners.comment: Bang-in-form filly who won from the front here (1m) and at Carlisle (7f) last month

runners.name: Novak
runners.spotlight: Second to an in-form subsequent winner over C&D (good to firm) last month and again ran well when close third at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) nine days ago; remains on a workable mark; must be considered.
runners.comment: Good second over C&D last month and again ran well over 6f this month; must be considered

runners.name: Al Muqdad
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who returned to turf with C&D third (good to firm; just behind Navak) last month, form that has been well advertised by the winner since; won't mind what the weather does; ought to be in the shake-up.
runners.comment: Returned to turf with good C&D third last month and won't mind what the weather does

runners.name: The Gay Blade
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 7yo who completed a 7f Carlisle double on Monday and has now won five times this year; has 4lb penalty to carry but he won't mind if the ground eases; commands respect in current mood.
runners.comment: Completed 7f Carlisle double on Monday and has now won five times this year; respected

runners.name: Imperial Guard
runners.spotlight: Safely held on both starts this season, latterly after setting a fast pace and refusing to settle in a first-time visor (now removed) over 6f a fortnight ago; should be fine back over 7f, though, and is too well handicapped to rule out.
runners.comment: Refused to settle when tried in visor last time; too well handicapped to rule out, though

runners.name: Ashen
runners.spotlight: Placed over 1m at Musselburgh (good) in April and justified strong market support when scoring over C&D (good to firm) last month, his fifth start for Jim Goldie; not sure to be aided by the forecast rain but still features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D last month, his fifth run for Jim Goldie, and goes on the shortlist

runners.name: Dorney Lake
runners.spotlight: Gained all four wins over 6f on good or faster ground; shaped as though worth another crack at 7f when keeping-on fifth over the stiff 6f at Pontefract (good; first-time cheekpieces) 11 days ago but others appeal more all the same; tongue-tie refitted.
runners.comment: Four-time 6f winner; worth another crack at 7f but others appeal more all the same
runners.owner: Mrs C M Clarke, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Black Storm
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his Irish AW maiden win in 2024 but has made the frame on all three appearances this season, most recently when very close second at Musselburgh (7f, good) a fortnight ago; only 1lb higher here and should cope if the ground eases; likely contender.
runners.comment: Went very close at Musselburgh two weeks ago and is a likely contender again here

runners.name: Defence Missile
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden who was unplaced all six starts for this stable last season; returns from ten-month absence with lots to prove.
runners.comment: Unplaced all six starts for this stable last season; returns from ten-month break

runners.name: Catalyse
runners.spotlight: Well down the field on all four starts for Jim Goldie this season (6f-7f), most recently in refitted cheekpieces at Hamilton on Wednesday; stable has more obvious chance with Ashen.
runners.comment: Out of form for new stable this year, including in refitted cheekpieces on Wednesday

runners.name: Obito
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D (good; also suited by soft) last summer and left his low-key reappearance run behind when keeping-on second at Hamilton (1m1f, good) a fortnight ago; still on a workable mark; live claims.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D last summer and is still on a workable mark after recent Hamilton second

runners.name: Star Cast
runners.spotlight: Took form to whole new level at Chester (1m2f, soft; also suited by good) a week ago, storming 7l clear, and has now won five times this year; 3lb well in under 5lb penalty; obvious contender.
runners.comment: Stormed clear on soft ground at Chester a week ago; 3lb well in under penalty

runners.name: Sanafi Zabeel
runners.spotlight: Ex-German 5yo who came good for Jim Goldie at the third attempt, just holding on to make all at Hamilton (8.3f, good to firm) last month; ought to be suited by this move back up in trip and further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Just held on to make all at Hamilton (8.3f) last month and remains lightly raced

runners.name: Pol Roger
runners.spotlight: Yet to add to his two C&D wins in spring 2024 but got back in the groove with a close second at Pontefract (1m2f, good) this month, his second run this season, and remains well handicapped on some of last year's placed form; a possible.
runners.comment: Back in the groove with close second at Pontefract this month, and well suited by this C&D

runners.name: Dwindling Funds
runners.spotlight: Won four times here (7f-1m, good to firm-heavy) last year and showed career-best form when second in valuable Musselburgh handicap (1m1f, good) on seasonal debut in April; two lesser efforts have followed (1m-1m4f) but he still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Four course wins in 2025; placed in valuable race this year; enters calculations
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Livonian
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only six runs but has struggled on both starts for new stable this season (1m1f/7f); big leap of faith required to support him.
runners.comment: Unexposed after only six runs but has struggled on both starts for new stable this season

runners.name: Cisco Disco
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a revival in second half of last season, winning four times here (1m-1m2f, good-heavy); well down the field on last month's reappearance outing, though, and remains on a tough mark.
runners.comment: Won four times in second half of last season but made low-key return; still on tough mark

runners.name: Mr Lincoln
runners.spotlight: Behind Obito when sixth of eight at Hamilton (1m1f, good) this month but wasn't beaten far, then ran well over hurdles soon afterwards; rain will aid his cause here; considered each-way.
runners.comment: Recently ran well over hurdles; rain will aid his cause; considered each-way

runners.name: Spanish Hustle
runners.spotlight: Now 2lb lower than for second of last season's two wins at favourite track Hamilton but well beaten after racing far too freely on both outings this term; wears cheekpieces for first time since 2yo season; risky.
runners.comment: Well beaten after racing far too freely on both outings this season; cheekpieces refitted

runners.name: Molinari
runners.spotlight: In fairly good form on AW at Newcastle towards end of last year, making the frame over 1m2f on three of his last four appearances, but has fitness to prove after six-month absence.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in fairly good form on AW but has fitness to prove after six-month absence
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs G F Hall Racing

runners.name: Hunterian
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; placed on both turf starts this season, latterly when close second at Hamilton (8.3f, good to soft) nine days ago; back in action before 2lb nudge takes effect and might still have a bigger performance in him; good chance.
runners.comment: 2lb well in after recent second and not fully exposed after only six runs; good chance

runners.name: Approaching Dawn
runners.spotlight: Safely held off basement marks in first three handicaps but finished strongly from the rear to cause 22-1 surprise at Musselburgh (2m, good) a fortnight ago; only 1lb higher here and could quite plausibly have more to offer.
runners.comment: Finished strongly to cause 22-1 surprise at Musselburgh this month; remains lightly raced
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Golden Valour
runners.spotlight: Won off today's mark at Musselburgh (1m, good) last summer but went off the boil in the winter and has not been at his best on either start this year; younger rivals have more persuasive profiles.
runners.comment: Won off this mark last summer but not in same form again since; younger rivals preferred

runners.name: Mayor Of Maghera
runners.spotlight: Disappointing during four-race spell over hurdles for an Irish stable this year but belied 40-1 odds when keeping-on third for new yard at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) a fortnight ago; still well handicapped on older form and should be fine back at 1m; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Belied 40-1 odds when third on recent stable debut; still well treated on older form

runners.name: Walk On Walk On
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; seemed to respond well to first-time blinkers when fourth at Redcar (7f, good) last month, his third start for Noel Wilson, but suffered a heavy defeat a fortnight ago and now has it all to prove again; cheekpieces (worn once in April) tried again.
runners.comment: Ran well in new blinkers last month but well beaten since; too risky

runners.name: Carmel Valley
runners.spotlight: Unable to land a telling blow in three 7f Irish maidens but showed some promise and is in calmer waters for this stable/handicap debut; ought to be more competitive this time.
runners.comment: Safely held in three 7f Irish maidens but in calmer waters for stable/handicap debut

runners.name: Rory's Gold
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but put in pretty good front-running shift before fading into eighth of 18 at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) on Sunday, his second start for Simon Waugh; could have a say.
runners.comment: 0-8 but ran quite well from the front in a big field on Sunday; could have a say

runners.name: Flying Moonlight
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing more than glimmers of promise in three novice races for Gary Rutherford last year (6f-7f) but makes seasonal/stable/handicap debut from a basement mark and is worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: Showed only glimmers of promise as a 2yo but worth market check on stable/handicap debut

runners.name: Braes Of Doune
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent 8yo who tends to race a bit too freely and is usually ridden patiently; suited by this C&D and quite versatile ground-wise but absent since slightly disappointing run 13 months ago.
runners.comment: Largely consistent 8yo; absent 13 months and entitled to benefit from this outing

runners.name: Laravie
runners.spotlight: Won four in a row over hurdles at around this time last year and has collected three Flat wins this spring (1m6f-2m1f); still ahead of the handicapper under 4lb penalty for Pontefract success (2m1f, good) 11 days ago but was beaten at skinny odds in three-runner race at Yarmouth yesterday.
runners.comment: Three wins this spring; beaten at skinny odds on three-runner race yesterday

runners.name: Gallus Norman
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo who has form figures of 213 since switched to handicaps in April, the win gained by over 5l at Musselburgh (2m, good); seems to enjoy making the running, and may still have more improvement to come.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 7yo who has form figures of 213 since switched to handicaps; respected

runners.name: Aighear
runners.spotlight: Well held when third of four in C&D contest that was seemingly stolen from the front by a stablemate last month but it's unwise to judged her harshly on that; won here twice in the mud in the autumn and significant rain will give her strong claims.
runners.comment: Won here twice in the mud in the autumn; significant rain will give her strong claims

runners.name: Angelardo
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle (2m, AW) in March and went close over that C&D last month but was well held when fourth behind Laravie at Pontefract (2m1f, good) 11 days ago and still needs to prove himself on turf.
runners.comment: AW winner in March but well held when back on turf this month; others preferred

runners.name: La Tosca
runners.spotlight: No threat to all-the-way winner when second on handicap debut at Catterick (1m4f, good to firm) three weeks ago but that still goes down as an encouraging run, and Charlie Johnston is evidently keen to move this stoutly bred filly up in trip.
runners.comment: No threat to winner when 2nd on handicap debut (1m4f) but bred to improve over this trip
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Fleur De Mer
runners.spotlight: Without a win since 2023 but had a solid AW campaign before staying on for close third in first-time cheekpieces at Pontefract (1m4f, good to soft) on turf return in April; should be fine over today's longer trip; can make her presence felt.
runners.comment: Had solid AW campaign before close third in new cheekpieces on turf return in April
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs G F Hall Racing
